summary: Multi-agent rating and review of the Nibli source code (book-described parts only)
logs: Fanning out 12 unit reviewers + 4 cross-cutting auditors
126 raw findings collected; triaging to max 14
Judges: Ana — staff Rust engineer=4, Dr. Okafor — logic-programming implementer (Prolog/Datalog engines=4, Sam — production-infrastructure and security engineer; judges deployability: sandboxing=4

units.unit: gerna lexer + grammar (lexer.rs, preprocessor.rs, grammar.rs + grammar/{sentence,selbri,sumti}.rs, lib.rs flattener)
units.one_line: A clean, well-documented recursive-descent Lojban front end whose happy path is solid, but probe testing confirmed four silent-corruption defects (lexer truncation, flattener index corruption, mispaired connective recovery, depth-guard leak) that directly violate the zero-hallucination firewall contract the book stakes on this unit.
strengths: Zero-copy pointer-arithmetic span tracking end to end: token &str slices borrowed from the original input drive exact line:column error reporting (grammar.rs:109-148) and contiguity-verified post-lex merges (lexer.rs:117-191), with defensive guards for out-of-range pointers from test-constructed tokens
Preprocessor is a genuinely clean O(n) single pass resolving si/sa/su/zo/zoi/zei with thorough edge-case unit tests (no-match fallback, sa at end-of-stream, chained zei, empty stream)
Consistent save/restore backtracking discipline across ~20 try_parse_* functions; failed speculative parses reliably rewind

Substantial test volume for the unit: 186 grammar + 36 pipeline + 12 flattener tests plus lexer/preprocessor suites, with reusable helpers (parse_ok/parse_err, token constructors) and real-corpus parse tests (readme.lojban lines)

weaknesses.issue: Silent input truncation on lex error: tokenize's `while let Some(Ok(token))` stops at the first unlexable character (digits, commas, most punctuation, capitalized vowel-final words) and drops everything after it with ZERO errors. Probe: "mi klama 7 do prami .i lo gerku cu danlu" parses as one sentence, no errors — content silently lost, truncated prefix silently asserted into the KB. Also breaks zoi quotation of arbitrary non-Lojban payloads (the stated purpose of zoi), since payload characters kill the lexer before preprocessor.rs:217-239 ever sees them. Direct violation of the zero-hallucination/error-reporting contract.

Flattener cross-reference corruption: Abstraction (lib.rs:244) and Restricted (lib.rs:320) record `body_idx = sentences.len()` BEFORE recursing instead of using push_sentence's return value (as the Connected arm correctly does at lib.rs:146-147). Any abstraction/rel-clause body containing a nested sentence-pushing construct points at the wrong sentence. Probe confirmed: "mi djica lo nu lo gerku poi barda cu klama kei" produces an Abstraction whose body resolves to the "barda" rel-clause bridi instead of the "klama" bridi — wrong logical form emitted with no error. One-line fix.

Error recovery mispairs afterthought sentence connectives: after a failed sentence, `.i ja B` pops the last SUCCESSFULLY parsed sentence (an unrelated earlier one) as the left operand. Probe confirmed: "mi klama .i lo .i ja do prami" yields Connected(Ja, "mi klama", "do prami") — the valid standalone assertion is replaced by a disjunction, and lasna asserts it with only a warning. The adjacent fallback (grammar.rs:401-404) pushes the right operand standalone when no left exists, unconditionally asserting B even under ja/jo/jonai semantics. Recovery should drop the connective sentence and error, never re-pair or strip connectives.

Recursion depth guard leaks across error recovery: enter()/leave() do not pair on `?` early-return paths (e.g. sentence.rs:51,60, selbri.rs forwarding), and parse_text never resets self.depth between recovered sentences. Probe confirmed: 41 malformed sentences followed by a valid "mi klama" yields 11 spurious "maximum nesting depth exceeded" errors and the valid sentence FAILS to parse. Defeats the per-sentence recovery guarantee for batch loads (:load of a corpus with bad lines). Fix: reset depth in the recovery path or use an RAII guard.

Tanru built right-grouping (units folded from the right) while CLL specifies tanru group from the LEFT: "sutra gerku zdani" should be ((sutra gerku) zdani) but produces Tanru(sutra, Tanru(gerku, zdani)). Deliberately documented (ast.rs:211) but contradicts the book's one-true-parse-tree-per-CLL determinism claim; currently masked only because smuni's intersective event decomposition flattens grouping entirely.

The headline line:column pointer-arithmetic feature has no test asserting actual line/column values anywhere in the crate; test_parse_error_has_position_info asserts only that the error message is non-empty. Similarly, no test exercises lexer error behavior (truncation went unnoticed), nested abstraction+rel-clause flattening (misindex unnoticed), or depth behavior across recovery — the four confirmed bugs all sit exactly in the untested gaps.

Malformed be/bei clauses silently reinterpreted instead of rejected: "klama be" with no following sumti consumes `be` and returns the bare core; `bei` with no sumti is silently skipped. Inconsistent with the firewall posture of rejecting ambiguous/incomplete input (smuni rejects ambiguous rel clauses, but the parser swallows these).

Selma'o table misuses real Lojban class names in a codebase teaching selma'o classification: mi/do/ti are labeled COI (the vocative class; they are KOhA), terminators ku/kei/vau/ku'o/ke'e are lumped into one KU class, ro/su'o into PA. Internally consistent for sa-erasure, but ".i sa .i" (CLL's canonical erase-last-sentence) falls back to single-word erase because Pause is unclassifiable (preprocessor.rs:202-205), deviating from documented sa semantics.

units.standout: The zero-copy provenance design: every token is a borrowed &str slice of the original input from lexer through preprocessor to parser, so byte offsets recovered by pointer arithmetic give exact line:column error positions (grammar.rs:109-148) and contiguity-verified compound-cmavo merging/splitting (lexer.rs:117-191) with no span bookkeeping and no allocation — including defensive handling for slices that do not point into the original input (test-constructed tokens) and for whitespace gaps that must block merges.

units.one_line: A cleanly-architected arena-tree-to-flat-buffer layer with good documentation and a sound boundary design, undermined by a confirmed index-corruption bug that silently miscompiles any nested sentence body, and by book performance claims the code only half implements.
strengths: The flat AstBuffer design (nibli-types/src/ast.rs): typed parallel arrays with u32 indices, explicit roots vs non-root sentences, no pointers across the WASM boundary — the right representation for component interop, and the roots/non-roots invariant is documented and unit-tested.

ParseResult { buffer, errors } API: per-sentence error recovery with partial results plus line:column errors, covered by tests for mid-stream recovery, all-fail, empty input, and line/column reporting (pipeline_tests.rs:505-564).
Corpus regression gates: readme.lojban, gdpr.lojban, and drug-interactions.lojban are include_str! tested line-by-line (pipeline_tests.rs:569-660), pinning the parser to the shipped book artifacts.
ast.rs is thoroughly documented — every enum variant carries a doc comment naming the Lojban construct it models, making the AST readable to non-Lojbanists.

weaknesses.issue: CONFIRMED soundness bug: rel-clause flattening records body_idx = sentences.len() BEFORE push_sentence and discards push_sentence's returned index. If the clause body contains any nested sentence (e.g. an abstraction), body_sentence points at the nested sentence instead of the body. End-to-end probe: 'lo gerku poi ke'a nelci lo nu mi klama cu barda' flattens with the restrictor pointing at 'mi klama' instead of 'nelci ...' — silent miscompilation fed to smuni, breaking the flat-buffer contract. Fix is one line: use the return value (the Sentence::Connected arm at lib.rs:146-147 already does this correctly).

Same discarded-return-value bug in the Abstraction arm: any abstraction body that is a Connected sentence or contains a nested abstraction gets the wrong sentence index. 'lo nu nelci lo nu klama kei kei' flattens with BOTH Abstraction nodes pointing at 'klama' (probe-confirmed: Abstraction((Nu,0)) twice, the 'nelci' body orphaned); since abstraction bodies are parsed via parse_sentence() (grammar/selbri.rs:165), 'lo nu ganai broda gi brode kei' silently becomes an abstraction over just 'broda' — no nesting required to trigger.

Flattener unit tests never assert index integrity for nested sentence bodies, never flatten a Sentence::Connected at all, and skip tense/attitudinal/Tagged/Tanru/WithArgs arms; the corpus pipeline tests only check errors.is_empty() with no buffer assertions. The confirmed bug sits exactly in this untested gap — the tests check counts (sentences.len, roots.len) but not which index points where for the body-in-body case.

The book/CLAUDE.md arena claims are only fractionally implemented: arena covers tree edges (&'arena T) but every Root/ProSumti/Name/QuotedLiteral still heap-allocates a std String (no bumpalo alloc_str) and Bridi/WithArgs/Compound hold std Vecs (ast.rs:145,154,209,227,268-270); the claimed 'memory reuse via Bump::reset() for batch corpus processing' does not exist anywhere and is impossible through the API because the Bump is created inside parse_text_native and dropped per call.

Leftover scaffold/instruction comments shipped in production source: lib.rs:178-182 ('In the Flattener's top-level loop, ensure you are calling it like this: ...') and lib.rs:380-381 ('Add these tests to parser/src/lib.rs or a new integration test file.'), plus the entire parse_text_native body mis-indented at 8 spaces (lib.rs:28-52) — signals unreviewed generated/pasted code in a unit claiming production engineering.

unreachable!() panic on QuantifiedDescription with non-Lo/Le gadri: the invariant is enforced only in a distant grammar module (grammar/sumti.rs:356) while the AST type admits all five Gadri variants; a future grammar change converts this into a WASM trap (component abort) instead of a typed NibliError, contradicting the typed-error contract the book stakes Part IV on.

parse_text_native takes input: String by value but only ever borrows it; both call sites (lasna/src/lib.rs:607, nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:335) pay a forced .to_string() copy per parse — a needless allocation in the exact path the arena work was meant to optimize.

Systemic enum-mapping duplication in the flattener: the ast→flat Connective match is hand-repeated three times (lib.rs:159-164, 233-238, 348-353) and Gadri twice (lib.rs:270-276, 363-367); From impls would eliminate ~40 lines and the drift hazard between copies.

units.standout: The flat AstBuffer boundary representation: typed parallel arrays (selbris/sumtis/sentences) addressed by u32 indices with an explicit roots list distinguishing top-level sentences from embedded clause/abstraction bodies — a simple, WIT-compatible, pointer-free design that makes the WASM component boundary cheap and the downstream smuni consumer straightforward, with the roots/non-roots invariant both documented in code and pinned by dedicated unit tests.

units.unit: smuni semantic compiler (gadri, event decomposition, relative clauses, connectives, abstractions) — smuni/src/semantic.rs, semantic/{compile,helpers,selbri}.rs, dictionary.rs
units.one_line: The documented core paths (lo/le/la, ro→ForAll, Neo-Davidsonian decomposition, tanru shared event, stacked-poi conjoin, firewall) work as the book claims and are well regression-tested, but composition corners reachable from surface Lojban silently drop meaning — relative clauses on names/le vanish, connected sumti under fa/BAI tags lose their right operand, and ma-quantifier scoping is wrong under poi — which is disqualifying for a self-described zero-hallucination soundness engine.
strengths: The implicit-ke'a ambiguity firewall is principled fail-closed design: count_unspecified_predicates + inject_variable (helpers.rs:226-266) refuses to guess referent binding and the stacked-poi conjoin fix (helpers.rs:257-265) preserves it — verified by targeted regression tests that fail pre-fix.

~90 behavior-focused unit tests in semantic.rs assert FOL structure (quantifier shape, role-predicate args, shared event variables) rather than strings, with a regression test pinning every documented bug fix; dictionary.rs has its own arity spot-checks.

Clean IR boundary: Spur-interned tree IR resolved to strings exactly once at the WIT flattener (lib.rs:63-194), with Biconditional/Xor as first-class IR nodes expanded clone-free into shared sub-tree DAG indices (lib.rs:164-193).

Neo-Davidsonian event decomposition is uniform across Root/Converted/Negated/Grouped/Compound selbri and the tanru arm genuinely shares one event variable between modifier and head (selbri.rs:52-98), resolving the intersective fallacy as the book claims — confirmed by tests and pipeline probes.

weaknesses.issue: Relative clauses on sumti that introduce no quantifier (la names, le descriptions, pro-sumti) are SILENTLY DISCARDED: the restrictor attach and the firewall error-push are both inside `if let Some(last) = quants.last_mut()`, so when quants is empty the compiled rel body is dropped with no error. Verified: `la .adam. poi gerku cu klama` compiles to bare klama(adam) and asserts cleanly; `le gerku poi barda cu klama` likewise loses barda. Queries answer a more general question than asked — direct violation of the zero-hallucination contract, and exactly the silent-drop class the recent firewall commit claimed to eliminate.

Connected sumti nested under place tags, modal tags, or be/bei bound args silently drop the connective and right operand: resolve_sumti's Connected arm resolves only the left child. Verified: `fa mi .e do klama` compiles to klama(mi) only (do gone); `mi klama ri'a do .e ti` yields rinka(do,mi) with ti gone. The top-of-compile_bridi Connected expansion (compile.rs:25-72) only matches when the term itself is Connected, and gerna's try_parse_sumti wraps full sumti (including Connected) under Tagged/ModalTagged, so this is reachable surface Lojban. Silent meaning loss, no diagnostic.

ma quantifier scoping bug: ma_vars is compiler-level state drained at the end of EVERY compile_bridi (compile.rs:254), so a `ma` resolved in the outer bridi gets existentially closed by the nested compile_bridi of a later relative-clause body. Verified on `ma nelci lo gerku poi barda`: output is Exists(_v1, And(Exists(_v0, restrictor…), …nelci_x1(_ev1, _v0)…)) — the ∃_v0 for ma scopes only over the barda restrictor while the main-body occurrence of _v0 is free. Breaks witness extraction for the flagship ?? find queries whenever ma combines with poi.

Tanru event decomposition skips role predicates whose arg is Unspecified (the `continue` at selbri.rs:74 and :86), while Root's event_decompose emits all roles — violating the documented invariant ('all role predicates emitted … for inject_variable compatibility'). Consequence: a tanru as relative-clause body has no _x1 role for the firewall to count, so count==0 and valid unambiguous Lojban is rejected. Verified: `lo gerku poi sutra bajra cu klama` → '[Semantic Error] … no unambiguous subject (x1) slot'. Fail-closed but a false rejection from an internal inconsistency between the two decomposition paths.

rel_clause.kind is never read: noi (incidental, non-restrictive) and voi (non-veridical) compile identically to poi as domain restrictors. Verified: `ro lo gerku noi barda cu klama` produces byte-identical FOL to the poi version, turning 'all dogs (who are, incidentally, big) go' into 'all big dogs go' — a meaning change on assertion, and voi's subjective description is treated as a veridical restrictor.

da/de/di existential closure only scans the main bridi's arg list (compile.rs:244-252); a da reaching the form through a BAI modal sumti or a be/bei bound arg is left as a free variable, contradicting the module doc ('bare logic variables are wrapped in Exists'). Verified: `mi klama ri'a da` → rinka(Var da, mi, zo'e) with da unbound; `mi nelci lo gerku be da` → gerku_x2(_ev1, Var da) unbound. Free variables flow into logji's assertion path with undefined semantics.

FA place-tag handling silently loses arguments and deviates from CLL: a tag beyond the predicate's arity drops the term with no diagnostic (`if idx < target_arity` else nothing — verified `fu do gerku` loses do entirely while its description quantifiers would still be hoisted), and untagged sumti after a tagged one fill the first free slot rather than continuing from the tagged place (verified `fe do mi klama` puts mi→x1; CLL place-counter semantics gives x3).

The 5-arm quantifier-closure match (Universal/Existential/ExactCount/UniversalLe/ExactCountLe) is duplicated nearly verbatim (~80 lines) between compile_bridi and the WithArgs arm of apply_selbri — the recent stacked-poi fix had to be reasoned about in both places; and the connected-selbri arity-mismatch warning goes to eprintln instead of self.errors (selbri.rs:230-236), so API/WASM consumers never see it, while compile_ast surfaces only errors.first() (lib.rs:43).

units.standout: The implicit-ke'a ambiguity firewall (helpers.rs:226-266): count_unspecified_predicates targets exactly the Neo-Davidsonian _x1 role slots, inject_variable binds the described entity only when there is precisely one candidate, and anything else is rejected with an actionable 'use explicit ke'a' error instead of a guess — fail-closed semantics that genuinely embodies the zero-hallucination thesis, with the stacked-poi conjoin fix (And-merging restrictors while preserving the firewall) pinned by a regression test that fails pre-fix.

units.unit: smuni logic IR + flattener (smuni/src/ir.rs, smuni/src/lib.rs; quantifier closure in smuni/src/semantic/compile.rs)
units.one_line: A small, clean tree-IR-to-flat-buffer flattener whose clone-free Biconditional/Xor expansion is correct and verifiably DAG-safe downstream, but the unit's claimed quantifier closure inverts da/de/di scope (unsound) and the Xor expansion is silently unassertable end-to-end, with zero direct unit tests on the flattener itself.
strengths: Clone-free Biconditional/Xor expansion at the flattening boundary is logically correct ((¬A∨B)∧(¬B∨A); (A∨B)∧¬(A∧B)) and shares operand sub-trees by u32 index — verified read-only-safe against every downstream consumer (collect_exists_for_skolem is idempotent via contains_key; rule registration is positional, not node-id-keyed)

Strict topological invariant (children always pushed before parents) is maintained across all 16 node-push sites, making the flat buffer trivially safe to consume across the WASM boundary with no pointers

Clean boundary discipline: lasso::Spur interning inside the IR, strings resolved exactly once at the WIT boundary; root-only compilation with a precise comment explaining why rel-clause bodies in ast.sentences are not roots (lib.rs:30-32)
Biconditional expansion deliberately emits the Or(Not(P),Q) shape that logji's material-conditional recognizer matches, so .i jo / go...gi reason in both directions — pinned by integration tests

weaknesses.issue: Quantifier closure for da/de/di inverts scope: the ∃ wrap (compile.rs:244-252) runs AFTER the description-quantifier loop (compile.rs:157-241), so `ro lo gerku cu citka da` compiles to ∃da.∀x instead of Lojban's left-to-right ∀x.∃da. Empirically confirmed: assertion Skolemizes da to a plain shared constant (`da ↦ sk_2`, not ∀-dependent) — the engine asserts the strictly stronger "one thing all dogs eat", violating the zero-hallucination contract. NEW, not in todo.md.

Xor expansion And(Or(A,B), Not(And(A,B))) is unrepresentable by logji's assertion path: collect_ground_facts skips Or-without-Not and Not nodes, register_ground_material_conditional matches neither conjunct, so asserting `A .i ju B` ingests zero facts/rules yet returns success with a fact id — and querying the identical sentence back returns False (empirically confirmed). Eager expansion at flattening erases the Xor variant so downstream cannot even reject it. Bare `.i ja` disjunctive assertions share the same silent-loss fate. NEW.

Zero direct unit tests for the flattener: lib.rs has no test module; all 89 smuni tests target the tree IR or dictionary. The headline feature — Biconditional/Xor index-sharing expansion — has no structural assertion on emitted LogicNode shape/indices anywhere; Biconditional is covered only transitively via nibli-engine integration, Xor is covered by no test at any level (verified by grep).

compile_ast surfaces only errors.first() and discards the rest, and semantic errors are bare Strings with no position info — asymmetric with the parse-side typed-error contract (SyntaxDetail carries line:column); multi-sentence corpus loads report one semantic error per round-trip.

compile_from_gerna_ast takes AstBuffer by value but only ever borrows it (immediately delegates to private compile_ast — a pure pass-through wrapper adding a second name for the same operation), forcing callers to surrender or clone the AST.

flatten_form is unboundedly recursive (depth scales with chained sentence connectives / nested abstractions); pathological input traps the WASM component via stack exhaustion instead of returning the typed nibli-error the production contract promises. Mitigated by the wasmtime sandbox, but the failure mode is a trap, not an error.

Systemic boilerplate: the `let id = nodes.len() as u32; nodes.push(...); id` pattern is hand-repeated 16 times across 130 lines; a 3-line push_node helper would halve the function and remove the one class of copy-paste error (wrong child id) the unit is most exposed to.

units.standout: The clone-free connective expansion strategy itself: deferring Biconditional/Xor desugaring from the tree IR (where operands would need deep clones) to the flattening boundary where operands are already u32 indices, so each operand sub-tree is emitted once and shared by index in both expansion arms — and the emitted Or(Not(P),Q) shape is co-designed with logji's material-conditional recognizer so biconditionals get dual modus-ponens rules for free. The implicit children-before-parents topological invariant is held perfectly across all push sites, including the two multi-node expansions.

units.unit: logji knowledge base + fact store (logji/src/kb.rs, logji/src/fact_store.rs, logji/src/wasi_fact_store.rs, retract/rebuild in logji/src/lib.rs)
units.one_line: A well-documented typed fact store with structural unification and a registry+rebuild retraction architecture, undermined by two probe-confirmed soundness bugs in incremental retraction and a WASI persistence backend that is unreachable through its own trait.
strengths: Typed GroundTerm/StoredFact layer with structural unification (unify_facts) and Cow-based zero-copy substitution (substitute_term) cleanly replaces the old string-interner representation; f64-as-bits gives consistent Hash/Eq for numeric terms (kb.rs:92-379)

Retraction architecture is the right shape: per-assertion FactRecord registry, conservative fallback to full rebuild whenever forward rules, Skolems, or compiled rules are involved (lib.rs:132-265), with du-equivalence rebuilt from surviving facts

KnowledgeBaseInner has every one of its ~30 fields documented, and the hand-written Clone impl deliberately resets transient state (rebuilding, current_assertion_id, forward_depth), giving with_assumptions correct snapshot semantics (kb.rs:439-537)

Broad behavioral test coverage for this unit: dedicated suites for retraction (10+ tests), arg-position index, du union-find rebuild, integrity constraints, sorts, unification edge cases, and tabling invalidation (logji/src/tests.rs, 232 tests)

weaknesses.issue: NEW, probe-confirmed: duplicate ground assertions are conflated — the HashSet store tracks no multiplicity, so retracting one of two registry records asserting the same ground fact (reachable via REPL :assert / WIT assert-fact) removes the fact entirely; query returns False while list_facts still shows an active record asserting it. Incremental and rebuild paths disagree on the result.

NEW, probe-confirmed: incremental retraction collects leaves only from roots.first(), while assertion processes all roots — facts from later roots of a multi-root ground buffer survive retraction, leaving stale provable facts (post-retraction hallucination, directly against the belief-revision soundness claim).

NEW: the WASI backend is unusable through the FactStore trait the KB consumes — lookup_predicate/contains consult only the LRU cache, lazy loading needs the inherent load_predicate(&mut self) which is not on the trait and never called by the KB; a reopened store silently presents an empty KB (false negatives on every query). Its in-file tests call load_predicate directly, masking the hole; no production code constructs WasiFactStore, yet the book stakes Ch 12 on it.

NEW: WasiFactStore::remove deletes from cache/all_facts but leaves the offset in pred_index, so a removed fact resurrects after LRU eviction + reload; insert also appends duplicates to disk and unconditionally increments fact_count, so len() overcounts (and recounts duplicates on reopen).

FactStore trait design forces eager materialization: all_facts() -> &HashSet and &self lookup_predicate make a genuinely lazy/persistent backend unimplementable without violating the contract; clone_box on persistent stores silently drops unloaded facts, so hypothetical reasoning (with_assumptions) can run over a partial KB.

Assertion is non-atomic on error: if process_assertion fails mid-way (e.g. stratification rejection on a later root), facts/rules/entities from earlier roots remain in the KB but no FactRecord is registered — orphaned state that can never be retracted, only cleared by reset(); rebuild_inner likewise swallows per-buffer replay errors with `let _ =`.

extract_from_rules floods the candidate set with the entire domain (all entities + events + descriptions) whenever a rule conclusion has a PatternVar at the target position, defeating the predicate/arg-position indexes for any rule-derivable predicate; it also ignores its _var_name/_subs parameters. Likely contributor to the filed retract+rebuild non-termination (todo.md item 59 suspects candidate-set blowup).

reset() silently retains sort_hierarchy, entity_sorts, and traced_predicates — only integrity_constraints is documented as intentionally preserved; stale entity sorts produce spurious sort warnings against the next KB generation.

units.standout: The typed term/fact layer (kb.rs:92-379): GroundTerm with bit-pattern f64 hashing, StoredFact tense/deontic wrappers, structural unify_facts, and Cow-based substitute_term that allocates only when a substitution actually fires — a disciplined, zero-copy migration off string interning that makes the whole store hashable, indexable, and serde-ready for persistent backends in one coherent design.

units.one_line: The backward-chaining + proof-trace core of logji: an architecturally credible four-valued inference engine with a strong regression-test culture, but with two newly confirmed defects in the central query loop — an entailed formula can return definitive False, and a trivially reachable query stack-overflows the process.
units.standout: The definitive-only predicate-cache gating (logji/src/reasoning.rs:634-643): a precisely reasoned invariant — True/False are context-independent for a fixed KB while CycleCut/Depth verdicts depend on the visited stack and depth budget, so only the former are tabled — combined with cross-depth cache persistence for iterative deepening, and pinned by regression tests with exemplary doc comments explaining the exact poisoning scenario each prevents (test_tabling_cross_depth_persistence, test_cycle_cut_does_not_poison_sibling_conjunct, logji/src/tests.rs:5905-5972).
strengths: Four-valued QueryResult (True/False/Unknown(reason)/ResourceExceeded(kind)) propagated through conjunction/disjunction/negation with correct lattice semantics in the formula layer; NAF over Unknown stays Unknown (reasoning.rs:81-118), avoiding unsound negation of cycle-cuts

Tabling done right at the cache layer: definitive-only writes, clear-once-per-query, persist-across-deepening-passes, with invalidation on every assert/retract/rebuild (kb.rs:851-876, lib.rs:105/153/219)

Proof traces are a real DAG: ProofRef memoization with back-reference validation tests, short-circuit AND and witness-loop step truncation to prevent exponential trace blowup, and an honest EqualitySubstitution/PredicateNotFound fallback instead of the old unsupported holds:true leaf (reasoning.rs:1065-1171)

232 unit tests with dedicated sections for cycle cut, depth exhaustion, deepening, du-equivalence, and proof-rule structure; the best regression tests document the exact pre-fix failure mechanism in doc comments
units.unit: logji backward chaining + proof traces (logji/src/reasoning.rs, with query orchestration in logji/src/lib.rs and cache/unification support in logji/src/kb.rs)

weaknesses.issue: NEW, probe-confirmed soundness bug: `best_result = rule_pending.and_then(|r| prefer_non_definitive(best_result, r))` wipes an earlier rule's pending ResourceExceeded(Depth) whenever a later rule for the same conclusion fails definitively (rule_pending=None). The predicate returns definitive False, which is cached AND halts iterative deepening before the true proof depth. Probe: qqq→ppp + ttt→ppp + 3-hop chain to qqq makes entailed ppp(alis) return False (control without the competing rule returns True). Under NAF the wrong False becomes a wrong True. Same wipe replicated at lines 791, 937, 968.

NEW, probe-confirmed availability bug: the du-equivalence fallback recurses check_predicate_in_kb_typed(variant) with no cycle guard — try_backward_chain_typed removes its visited entry before returning and the variant loop neither inserts into visited nor increments depth, so P(a)→P(b)→P(a)→… recurses unboundedly. Probe: assert du(alis,bob), query unprovable mlatu(bob) ⇒ stack overflow, SIGABRT. Reachable via the documented `:assert du` REPL command / assert-fact WIT method; in native nibli-server (no fuel) this is a process-crash DoS. Traced fallback at 1110-1159 shares the root cause.

run_entailment_check_with_proof evaluates every root twice — once four-valued untraced, once boolean traced — and explicitly discards the traced verdict (`let _ = holds;`). Besides 2x cost per deepening pass, the reported QueryResult and the trace's holds flags can diverge (Unknown/ResourceExceeded results render as holds:false steps), undermining the Transparency Triad claim that the trace explains the verdict.

The rule-application machinery (unify → unbound-event-var candidate search → condition loop) is copy-pasted four times (bare/temporal-lifted x untraced/traced), ~550 near-identical lines that differ subtly in verdict strictness (untraced filters on !is_false(), traced on is_true()). The confirmed best_result wipe exists in all four copies; any fix must be applied in four places.

Traced CountNode drops the untraced version's unresolved-member handling: it counts only definitive True and treats Unknown/ResourceExceeded members as plain false, with no satisfying+unresolved bounds check (untraced has it at 237-264). The trace can present a definitive-looking CountResult while the engine correctly reports Unknown.

Mid-query compute auto-ingestion (assert_typed_fact on successful ComputeNode dispatch) mutates the fact store without invalidating the predicate cache, violating the documented invariant (kb.rs:871: 'call after any KB mutation'). A False cached for a rule-derived fact earlier in the same query stays authoritative even after ingestion makes it derivable; only direct assertions are rescued by the asserted-before-cache lookup order.

Proof memo entries for failures (PredicateNotFound) are keyed by display string and memoized despite being depth-context-dependent — a fact that failed near the depth boundary replays as a holds:false ProofRef when re-requested at shallow depth in the same trace, so a stale not-found leaf can appear under a Derived step that claims holds:true.

UnknownReason::NafDependent is part of the public type/WIT contract and matched for display (reasoning.rs:2039) but no reasoning path ever produces it — negated conditions just propagate the inner reason through negate_result. The four-valued story the contract advertises is partially dead code.

units.one_line: logji's universal-rule compiler and session layer — a cleanly architected typed-template/SkolemFn rule engine whose happy paths are solid and well-tested, but whose silent-drop handling of non-atomic condition atoms produces a confirmed, surface-reachable unsoundness (disjunctive antecedents derive their consequent unconditionally), plus a flaky stratification guard and duplicated core loops.
units.unit: logji universal rule compilation + session layer (logji/src/rules.rs, logji/src/lib.rs)
strengths: Typed rule-template design: condition-side existentials compile to pattern variables while conclusion-side existentials become SkolemFn/DepPair terms, matched by structural unify_facts — verified correct end-to-end on multi-hop chain control probes (rules.rs:571-814, kb.rs:254-325)
Stratification guard at rule-registration time with dependency-graph rollback on rejection, plus principled NAF gating of forward rules on negated conditions (rules.rs:232-380, 513-516)

Careful cache discipline: only definitive True/False results are tabled, non-definitive depth/cycle results stay context-local, and the cache is cleared per top-level query but persists across iterative-deepening passes (lib.rs:285-317, kb.rs:851-876)

Honest, intention-revealing comments throughout (e.g. the TODO(naf-forward) truth-maintenance caveat at rules.rs:531-533, the xorlo ground-conditional exemption rationale at rules.rs:723-726) and idiomatic Cow-based substitution avoiding clones of ground terms (kb.rs:349-379)

weaknesses.issue: CONFIRMED UNSOUNDNESS (new, reachable from surface Lojban): condition atoms the template builder cannot represent are silently dropped. A disjunctive antecedent `ganai ga A gi B gi C` decomposes to conditions=[Or(A,B)]; build_rule_template_fact_with_negation has no Or arm, returns None, and the loop skips it — registering a ZERO-condition rule that makes C unconditionally derivable. Probe verified: with neither disjunct asserted, the consequent queries True. Fix should reject the assertion when any condition atom fails to compile.

check_stratification has a nondeterministic false negative: `cycle_has_neg = *is_neg || (has_neg && !dep_neg)` loses the position of negative edges, so a cycle containing a negative edge is missed when the DFS path into the cycle already crossed a negative edge before the cycle entry (e.g. edges X→A neg, A→B pos, B→C neg, C→A pos with DFS starting at X). Detection depends on HashMap key iteration order, so the NAF-soundness guard silently fails on some runs.

best_result clobbering in the core chaining loop: `best_result = rule_pending.and_then(|r| prefer_non_definitive(best_result, r))` resets best_result to None when a later rule's conditions fail definitively, discarding an earlier rule's ResourceExceeded(Depth) pending. The resulting definitive False is cached cross-depth and halts iterative deepening; via negate_result a wrong False flips to a wrong True under NAF. Currently latent (per-rule SkolemFn conclusion names prevent competing unification through the surface pipeline) and copy-pasted into all four loop variants (also reasoning.rs:791, 937, 968).

Proof queries evaluate every root TWICE — full untraced check_formula_holds then check_formula_holds_traced — and discard the traced verdict (`let _ = holds;`). The traced path uses strict two-valued condition semantics (result.is_true()/is_false(), reasoning.rs:1257-1289) versus the untraced four-valued pending propagation, so the displayed proof tree can diverge from the reported verdict, undermining the proof-honesty contract; and all proof queries pay ~2x.

Tense wrappers are stripped while collecting universals and never recorded on the rule, so a past-tensed universal (`pu ku ro ...`) compiles to a timeless rule whose conclusions then fire for any tense via temporal lifting — contradicting the documented strict tense discrimination.

rebuild_inner clears predicate_registry, silently wiping user-declared arg_sorts from set_predicate_sorts after any retraction-triggered rebuild (entity_sorts/sort_hierarchy are inconsistently preserved), and swallows per-buffer replay errors with `let _ = process_assertion(...)`, allowing the rebuilt KB to silently diverge from the registry.

Rule dedup is a bare 64-bit DefaultHasher digest stored in HashSet<u64> with no equality confirmation — a hash collision silently discards a semantically distinct rule (assertion accepted, rule never registered). Astronomically unlikely but trivially avoidable for a prover claiming formal derivation guarantees.

Bare-universal branch uses `vec![inner_body_id]` instead of flatten_consequent, so a bare forall with a conjunctive/event-decomposed body compiles to a rule with ZERO conclusions, silently indexed under '__fallback__' — an inconsistency trap relative to the implication branch (currently shielded only because `ro da klama` fails to parse).

units.standout: The condition-side-∃-as-pattern-variables vs conclusion-side-∃-as-SkolemFn split in compile_forall_to_rule (rules.rs:626-694) combined with the typed GroundTerm template representation (PatternVar / SkolemFn / right-nested DepPair) unified structurally via unify_facts (kb.rs:254-325): it gets the asymmetric semantics of Neo-Davidsonian event variables in rule antecedents vs consequents exactly right, replaced an entire string-interning representation with a typed one, and was verified correct end-to-end by multi-hop chain probes — this is the semantic heart of the engine and it is genuinely well-engineered.

units.unit: lasna pipeline glue (session resource, compute dispatch registration) — lasna/src/lib.rs
units.one_line: Clean, idiomatic WIT-boundary glue with a well-built go'i snapshot subsystem, undermined by a verified session-corrupting lifecycle bug in that same subsystem and zero test coverage of the session surface itself.
strengths: Exhaustive, compiler-enforced match coverage everywhere (type conversions, snapshot visitors, rebase fns) — adding an AST/IR variant breaks the build instead of silently misbehaving
Single shared compile_pipeline helper keeps all five text entrypoints on one code path with uniform typed nibli-error propagation (Syntax with line:column, Semantic, Reasoning, Backend)

The SelbriSnapshot extract/rebase/graft machinery is genuinely clever: deep-clones an arbitrary selbri subtree (tanru, negation, conversion, abstractions, be/bei args) into self-contained vecs, then grafts via in-place index rebasing + Vec::append with zero node cloning
13 focused unit tests on the snapshot/graft/go'i helpers, including cross-call and no-antecedent error cases

weaknesses.issue: VERIFIED session-killing bug: graft_snapshot drains the stored snapshot's vecs via Vec::append, and only a successful assert_text refreshes last_relation. Every query path (query_text, query_find_text, query_text_with_proof, compile_debug) and the assert warnings-abort path grafts-and-drains without refreshing, so the NEXT go'i-containing call grafts empty vecs and resolve_go_i returns an out-of-bounds selbri index -> smuni indexing panic -> WASM trap. Reproduced empirically with a probe test (relation 1 vs selbris len 1). REPL repro: 'mi klama' / '? go'i' / '? go'i'.

go'i inside abstractions or relative-clause bodies is never resolved: resolve_sentence_go_i only inspects top-level bridi relations and Connected children, while gerna parses go'i in any selbri position (gerna/src/grammar/selbri.rs:192) and smuni has no go'i handling — so 'lo nu go'i' silently asserts a literal predicate named go'i, a hallucinated fact in a zero-hallucination engine.

Parse-warning contract is inconsistent: assert_text aborts on partial-parse warnings but all four query paths silently discard _warnings and reason over the partial parse — a multi-sentence query with one unparseable sentence returns an answer scoped to the parsed remainder with no indication. The native nibli-engine pipeline aborts the same query (nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:351-366), so the two runtime surfaces give different answers for identical input.

Pipeline policy logic is duplicated (verbatim abort message) and divergent between lasna's compile_pipeline and nibli-engine's compile_text: go'i support exists only on the WASM surface, warning policy differs for queries, and no integration test exercises lasna's Session at all — nibli-engine tests bypass this crate entirely.

Zero tests for the Session WIT surface, compile_pipeline, or the boundary conversions; the 13 unit tests cover only snapshot helpers. A single lifecycle test (assert, query-with-go'i, query-with-go'i) would have caught the major bug.

assert_fact (direct injection) overwrites last_relation BEFORE the fallible kb.assert_fact call, so a failed injection still clobbers the go'i antecedent — inconsistent with assert_text, which updates only on success.

The warnings-abort error reports line: 0, column: 0 even though parse_result.errors carries exact positions (used correctly at lib.rs:617-624) — undercuts the book's 'exact line:column reporting' claim for the typed error contract.
eval_via_host flattens typed compute-backend errors: drops the backend error kind on Backend((_, m)) and Debug-formats all other NibliError variants into user-visible strings ('{:?}').

units.standout: The SelbriSnapshot extract/rebase/graft machinery (lib.rs:247-536): a self-contained deep-clone of an arbitrary selbri subtree across three parallel index spaces (selbris/sumtis/sentences) with cycle-safe memoized visitors and placeholder-then-patch insertion, grafted back via in-place index rebasing plus Vec::append so re-injection costs zero node clones — exhaustive matches mean any new AST variant is a compile error, and it is the one part of the unit with real unit tests. (Ironically, its drain-on-graft optimization is also the source of the major bug — the mechanism is excellent, its lifecycle management is not.)

units.unit: gasnu Wasmtime host (gasnu/src/main.rs — REPL, fuel/memory limits, WASI sandbox, TCP compute client)
units.one_line: A feature-rich, well-commented Wasmtime host whose advertised core (per-command refuel, live StoreLimits, minimal WASI sandbox, lazy-reconnect JSON-Lines client) is genuinely implemented, but whose recovery and persistence paths harbor two serious defects: post-trap session reuse that can leave the guest KB poisoned, and engine/store fact-ID divergence that silently corrupts the persistent store.
strengths: Disciplined safety plumbing: refuel() is applied before every one of the 13 WASM-calling REPL branches, and the memory limit is live-updatable because store.limiter reads state.limits through a closure — :memory takes effect without recreating the store (main.rs:608-610, 689, 965-977)
WASI sandbox exactly matches the book's claim: inherit_stdout/inherit_stderr only — no stdin, filesystem, network, or env vars reach the component (main.rs:674-679)

Pipelined batch compute dispatch: evaluate_batch resolves builtins locally, writes all external requests in one burst with a single flush, reads responses in order, and stitches results back by original index — eliminating N-1 round trips (main.rs:162-203, 458-525)

Real unit tests with a live mock TCP server covering dispatch success/failure/error mapping, reconnect-after-drop, JSON wire format, and NibliError formatting — unusual rigor for a binary REPL crate (main.rs:1309-1926)

weaknesses.issue: Persistent-store fact-ID divergence corrupts the store: startup replay re-asserts via call_assert_text and discards the new engine IDs (logji fact_counter restarts at 0), while redb keys remain the original IDs. After any retraction gap or replay error, :retract tombstones the WRONG store record (retracted facts resurrect on next restart), and a new assert produces an engine ID colliding with a live store key — nibli-store insert_fact (table.insert) silently OVERWRITES the persisted fact. logji even has assert_fact_with_id built for this exact replay case, but the WIT session surface never exposes it and gasnu doesn't use it.

Post-trap session reuse: after a fuel or memory trap the REPL prints '[Limit] ... Increase with NIBLI_FUEL' and keeps calling the same session. Traps don't unwind the guest — logji holds a RefCell borrow across process_assertion, so a trap mid-assert leaves the borrow flag set in linear memory (every later call panics in-guest) and the KB half-mutated (unsound derivations possible). No component re-instantiation path exists; the friendly message implies retry is safe when it is not.

Host-error classification by substring: any trap message containing 'memory' (e.g. wasm 'out of bounds memory access' — a component bug) is misreported as 'Memory limit exceeded, increase NIBLI_MEMORY_MB', while a genuine StoreLimits denial surfaces as a guest allocator abort whose trap text likely contains neither 'fuel' nor 'memory', so the friendly message never fires for the real limit case. Should downcast to wasmtime::Trap (Trap::OutOfFuel exists). Tests validate the heuristic only against synthetic anyhow strings.

Retry-once conflates transport and application errors: try_dispatch returns Err for backend `{"error": ...}` responses, and dispatch_to_backend treats any Err as a connection failure — drops the healthy connection and re-sends the identical request (duplicate dispatch, pointless reconnect per unknown-predicate call), discarding _first_err so the user may see a different second error. Same pattern duplicated in evaluate_batch.

No connect timeout on the compute backend: TcpStream::connect (plus synchronous DNS resolution) blocks the single-threaded REPL for the OS default TCP timeout (can be minutes) on a black-holed address; read/write timeouts are carefully configured but the connect itself is unbounded — should use connect_timeout.

Unknown or bare colon-commands fall through to Lojban assertion: the prefix dispatch requires a trailing space, so bare ':load', ':debug', ':dump', ':assert' and any typo (':laod x') are passed to call_assert_text and produce a misleading '[Syntax Error]' instead of usage help or 'unknown command' — a production REPL should reject unrecognized ':'-prefixed input.

:merge KB rebuild counts engine-level assertion failures as success: `.is_ok()` checks only the outer host Result, so Ok(Err(NibliError)) increments 'replayed' and the error is silently dropped — facts vanish from the live KB while '[Merge] KB rebuilt with N facts' over-reports. Diverges from the careful three-way error handling in the startup replay whose logic it duplicates (~80 lines copy-pasted).

:dump/:load round-trip is broken for direct facts: the engine label for assert-fact is ':assert {relation}' WITHOUT arguments (lasna/src/lib.rs:756), so dumped lines permanently lose argument data, and :load only calls assert_text so ':assert ...' lines fail to parse as Lojban on reload. Related minor bookkeeping: try_dispatch_batch never updates backend_last_used, so the idle-timeout logic misfires for batch-only workloads.

units.standout: The pipelined batch compute dispatch (evaluate_batch + try_dispatch_batch, main.rs:162-203/458-525): builtins are short-circuited locally, the remaining requests are serialized once, written in a single burst with one flush, responses are read back in order, and results are merged back into the caller's slots by original index — a clean, allocation-conscious design that eliminates N-1 TCP round trips while preserving exact per-request error attribution, with the local/remote split kept invisible to the WASM guest.

units.one_line: The WIT component contract is a thoroughly documented, well-designed flat-buffer boundary, but the native NibliEngine wrapper around it ships a real persistence-soundness bug (retraction not persisted, replay errors silently dropped), erases the typed error contract into strings, and has drifted both from the WIT surface and from lasna's parallel pipeline glue.
units.standout: The wit/world.wit contract design: flat u32-indexed parallel-array buffers (ast-buffer, logic-buffer, proof-trace all use the same indices-not-pointers pattern, including proof-step.children as list<u32>), resource-based session/knowledge-base lifecycles, and a single shared nibli-error variant used across all five interfaces — every variant and tuple field carries a doc comment explaining its Lojban semantics, making the contract readable as a specification. The wrapper reinforces it with exhaustive match-based conversions (rule_to_json covers all 18 proof-rule variants; node_to_stored/from_stored cover all 13 node kinds), so any type drift between WIT mirror types, store types, and wire types becomes a compile error.
strengths: wit/world.wit is specification-grade: every interface, record, variant, and positional tuple field is doc-commented with its Lojban meaning and index convention; flat u32 buffers cleanly avoid pointer marshaling across WASM boundaries; typed nibli-error + session/knowledge-base resources are idiomatic component-model design
Exhaustive match-based type conversions in nibli-engine/src/lib.rs (rule_to_json, node_to_stored/node_from_stored) make cross-representation drift a compile error rather than a runtime surprise

The single unit test (persistent_assert_does_not_mutate_kb_when_store_is_unavailable, lib.rs:531) is a thoughtful failure-path atomicity test verifying neither KB nor store leak state on a failed persistent assert

The 41-test integration suite is genuine black-box coverage of the wrapper API: persistence replay/reopen, store-level retraction, CRDT merge-then-reopen, proof-trace JSON validity, and both case-study corpora (gdpr/ddi) pinned end-to-end including count_witnesses_text and aggregate_text

weaknesses.issue: Retraction is not persisted: NibliEngine::retract_fact only calls kb.retract_fact and never calls NibliStore::retract_fact (which exists at nibli-store/src/lib.rs:383), so on a persistent engine a retracted fact stays active on disk and replay_from_store resurrects it on reopen — belief revision silently undone across restarts. The integration test persistent_engine_honors_store_retractions_after_reopen (integration.rs:383) sidesteps the engine API and retracts via the store directly, leaving the broken engine-level path untested. assert_fact_direct (lib.rs:411) similarly bypasses the store, so direct facts are lost on reopen.

replay_from_store drops the Result<(), String> from assert_fact_with_id — facts that fail to replay vanish silently (active on disk, absent in memory) with no error or log; verified cargo check emits an unused_must_use warning here, contradicting the project's compiles-cleanly claim

The typed error contract the book stakes its production claim on is erased at the native embedding layer: every public wrapper method returns Result<_, String> via .map_err(|e| e.to_string()), so embedders (nibli-server) cannot match Syntax/Semantic/Reasoning/Backend without string sniffing; additionally lib.rs:400 wraps a logji assertion error in PipelineError::Semantic, mislabeling reasoning errors — and only on the persistent path, so the same failure reports different categories depending on persistence mode

compile_text duplicates lasna's compile_pipeline (lasna/src/lib.rs:594-637) with real behavioral drift: nibli-engine has no go'i/observative resolution (lasna does via resolve_go_i), and partial-parse policy differs — lasna query_text silently queries the partial buffer discarding warnings while nibli-engine aborts queries with an 'Assertion aborted' message; the same Lojban behaves differently in gasnu REPL vs server/UI

WIT surface drift: count-witnesses/aggregate (and an aggregate-op type) exist only on the native wrapper (count_witnesses_text/aggregate_text, lib.rs:457-475) and are absent from the logji and lasna WIT interfaces, so the book's aggregation case-study capability is unreachable from the WASM component contract (gasnu); contradiction-check and predicate tracing are likewise native-only

Doc rot inside the contract file: compute-backend interface doc advertises 'membership queries (for ∀/∃ over computed predicates)' but no such function exists (only evaluate and evaluate-batch); logical-term.description doc still says 'from le/la gadri' although la now compiles to constant (C5 change); parse-error (line 175) duplicates syntax-detail (line 210) field-for-field instead of reusing it

Inconsistent error typing within the compute-backend interface — evaluate returns result<bool, nibli-error> while evaluate-batch items use err(string); and positional tuples with bare bools (connected(tuple<sumti-id, connective, bool, sumti-id>) line 84, afterthought(tuple<bool, connective, bool>) line 150) rely on comments where named records would be self-documenting and misuse-resistant

Library hygiene: unconditional println! to stdout in new()/open()/replay_from_store (lines 270/296/317) with no logging facade or quiet option, polluting every embedder including servers and tests; the partial-parse abort discards the first error's real line/column, hardcoding 0,0 (lines 363-364) though parse_result.errors[0] carries coordinates; .map(|id| id) no-ops at lines 406 and 423; reset() silently skips clearing the persistent store if the RefCell is borrowed (lines 376-380)

units.unit: integration tests + case-study corpora (nibli-engine/tests/integration.rs, gdpr.lojban, drug-interactions.lojban, readme.lojban, gerna/src/pipeline_tests.rs)
units.one_line: A 41-test integration suite with genuinely disciplined negative controls and regression pins, undermined by the GDPR tests quietly avoiding the shipped corpus — which empirically cannot answer the chapter's queries in reasonable time — and DDI tests that enshrine an alert independent of the patient's actual regimen.
strengths: Discriminating negative controls throughout, not smoke: apixaban/CYP3A4 control checked into the corpus itself; two-sided conjunction controls in ddi_toxicity_requires_both_conditions; per-article GDPR negative controls; a full three-way matrix in stacked_poi_conjoins_both_clauses (integration.rs:641-664)
Real soundness regressions pinned by tests that failed pre-fix: stacked-poi conjoin, biconditional both-direction tests (go gi / .i jo), ganai with a no-antecedent control (integration.rs:237-286)

Honest-provenance contract enforced in tests: the NAF-derived erasure verdict must carry naf_dependent=true in the proof JSON (integration.rs:525-530), and assert_false demands a strict QueryResult::False, never merely not-True
API-coherence pinning: query_holds_matches_proof_query_boolean (integration.rs:300-314) guarantees the boolean and proof query paths cannot silently diverge

weaknesses.issue: The GDPR section banner claims 'the corpus file (gdpr.lojban) is the single source of truth; these tests pin the behaviour the chapter narrates so prose and engine cannot drift' — but only gdpr_file_loads_clean touches gdpr.lojban (assert-only, zero queries); all six gdpr_* reasoning tests rebuild 3-5 line inline KBs. Empirical probe: against the actually-loaded corpus, the chapter's lawful-basis query takes 3.3s and the breach-notification query (Ch20 narrates it verbatim) did not return within ~230s before timeout kill. Chapter 20 tells readers to ':load gdpr.lojban' and run these queries in the REPL; the test structure makes this non-reproducibility invisible and the pinning claim false. Not filed in todo.md — a new finding.

The DDI alert is independent of patient exposure: probe confirmed la .varfarin. cu kajde stays True after retracting the patient's ENTIRE regimen (patient takes nothing). The pilno facts (drug-interactions.lojban:59-60) never enter the mechanism chain (lines 71-85), yet the corpus header frames the question as 'should this co-prescription raise a safety alert?'. ddi_belief_revision_discontinue_inhibitor models 'prescriber discontinues fluconazole' by retracting the pharmacological constant 'fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9' instead of the prescription — a category error (enzyme inhibition is not contingent on prescribing) that the test and Chapter 21 both enshrine.

Persistence test fixtures leak and isolate only by accident: cleanup() removes <name>.redb but NibliEngine::open also creates <name>.typed.redb (lib.rs:286-288) which RedbFactStore::open eagerly reloads into the queryable fact store on the next run. Verified ~15MB of leftover DB files per run, growing across runs; retract_then_reopen.typed.redb retains the supposedly-retracted fact. Tests stay green only because in-memory entity registries are not persisted, making stale typed facts unreachable for event-decomposed queries — undocumented, fragile isolation.

Proof-trace pinning is substring-weak: the only derivation-structure assertions are trace.contains("Rule"), which also matches RuleAttemptFailed; no test pins which rule fired, the multi-hop chain shape (gerku→danlu→citka), or Derived/Asserted nesting for either case study, despite multi-hop provenance being a headline book feature (Ch 11).

readme.lojban — the documented REPL :load bootstrap prelude — is only parse-tested through gerna; no test asserts it through smuni+logji anywhere, so a semantic-compiler regression (e.g., the ambiguity firewall rejecting a line) stays green. todo.md itself flags line 107's polarity as unverified yet it ships untested at the pipeline level.

ddi_dose_sum_aggregation and ddi_temporal_alert_discrimination sit under the 'corpus is the single source of truth' banner but use inline KBs unrelated to the corpus (drug-interactions.lojban contains no klani facts; the temporal test duplicates temporal_tense_discrimination with renamed constants), padding the ddi_* count with tests that pin nothing corpus- or chapter-specific.

The corpus-loading loop is copy-pasted five times: three identical parse loops in pipeline_tests.rs and two assert loops in integration.rs; the GDPR side lacks the engine_with_*_corpus helper the DDI side has — exactly the missing abstraction that enabled the corpus-never-queried gap.

units.standout: The two-sided discriminating controls in ddi_toxicity_requires_both_conditions (integration.rs:762-796): a wide-margin drug WITH raised concentration and a narrow-index drug WITHOUT exposure — the precise pair that detects conjunction-degeneration in rule antecedents, the exact failure mode of the stacked-poi soundness bug the suite separately pins. Together with the apixaban negative control checked into the shipped corpus itself, this is negative-control engineering that makes tests genuine bug detectors rather than smoke.

units.unit: python compute backend + Transparency Triad server/UI (python/nibli_backend.py, nibli-server/src/main.rs, nibli-ui/src/main.rs — book-scoped parts)
units.one_line: Competent, ops-conscious glue: a clean stateless query-with-KB GraphQL resolver, a serviceable reference compute backend, and a working Dioxus proof UI — but the advertised production hardening is thinner than claimed (timeouts don't cancel unbounded native reasoning, the documented temperature-0.0 determinism is actually 0.3, and the hardening itself is untested).
strengths: Stateless queryWithKb resolver (nibli-server/src/main.rs:707-738): fresh NibliEngine per request with per-line KbStatus feedback makes the documented 'Lojban tab is the single source of truth' semantics trivially sound under concurrency, and composes correctly with logji's clear-at-query-start thread-local predicate cache (verified at logji/src/kb.rs:860-876) so tokio blocking-thread reuse cannot poison results

Production-grade ops surface: env-driven ServerConfig with loopback-aware safe defaults (playground/CORS auto-enabled only on loopback binds), lock-poison-aware /readyz, Prometheus /metrics, and monitoring routes deliberately exempt from rate limiting — all exercised end-to-end via axum oneshot tests (main.rs:2618-2699)

Consistent error-as-value discipline in resolvers: poisoned locks, broadcast failures, and partial successes ('local assert succeeded, but broadcast failed') are returned as structured GraphQL error fields rather than panics or unwraps on any user-facing path

Python backend tests (python/test_nibli_backend.py) spawn the real server subprocess and exercise the actual wire protocol: all five handlers, unknown relation, arity errors, division-by-zero, and persistent multi-request connections

weaknesses.issue: queryWithKb runs unbounded native reasoning on spawn_blocking with no fuel/memory limits (those exist only in the WASM/gasnu path); the 30s request-timeout middleware (main.rs:1573-1586) only abandons the response — blocking tasks cannot be cancelled, so given logji's known non-terminating queries (filed in todo.md), each pathological request returns 408 but permanently pins a blocking-pool thread at 100% CPU. The book's 'production hardening' does not bound work on the canonical Transparency Triad runtime; repeated requests exhaust the pool.

Determinism contract broken: book Ch 19 (P5_C19:30) and CLAUDE.md state Ollama 'temperature fixed at 0.0 for deterministic output', but the code sends temperature 0.3 — the book even leans on this pin in its hallucination-reproducibility argument.

Backend violates its own protocol on malformed input: only ValueError/TypeError/ZeroDivisionError are caught, so a non-dict JSON line (e.g. '[1,2]'), non-dict args entries (get_number(1) -> AttributeError at line 35), or tenfa overflow (1e200**2 -> OverflowError at line 83) escape the dispatch loop and kill the connection thread instead of returning the documented {"error": ...} response.

Book-scoped server surface is untested: the test module covers gossip paths and ops endpoints, but there is no test for queryWithKb, assert_kb_lines (comment/blank skipping, per-line error reporting), or translateToLojban, and the advertised hardening is never exercised — no 429 or 408 assertion anywhere (tests set rate_limit_rps to 1000 and never hit it).

Rate limiter is a single global NotKeyed bucket — any one client exhausts the quota and 429s every other client (a self-DoS amplifier rather than per-client protection); additionally NIBLI_SERVER_RATE_LIMIT_RPS=0 panics at startup via expect instead of a config error, unlike all other env parsing in ServerConfig::from_env.

Stale proof panel: when a query errors or returns no trace, proof_text/proof_data are not cleared, so the previous query's proof tree remains displayed next to the new result — misleading in a transparency-first UI; relatedly, a missing GraphQL status field silently renders as 'FALSE' (line 305) instead of an error.

ProofNodeView clones the entire ProofTrace for every rendered node (O(n^2) for large proof trees, exactly the deep memoized traces the engine produces) and recurses on child indices with no cycle guard or depth cap — a malformed/cyclic trace JSON from the server causes unbounded recursion in WASM.

translateToLojban builds a fresh reqwest::Client per request (no pooling) and sets no client-level timeout on the Ollama call — a hung Ollama holds the request until the generic 30s middleware fires; the connection-refused special-case is nice but the call deserves its own bound.

units.standout: The stateless queryWithKb resolver design (nibli-server/src/main.rs:707-738): by constructing a fresh NibliEngine per request and returning per-line KbStatus alongside the proof, it makes the book's 'every query resets the engine, re-asserts the Lojban tab, then queries' semantics structurally sound under concurrency — and it composes correctly with logji's thread-local predicate cache because that cache is cleared at every top-level query (logji/src/kb.rs:860-876), so tokio blocking-thread reuse across requests with different KBs cannot poison results. This is the one place where a sharp cross-layer hazard was designed away rather than patched.

audits.one_line: A deterministic Lojban-to-FOL backward-chaining engine whose four-valued reasoning core and cache discipline are genuinely solid, but whose rule-compilation layer fails open: probes reproduced three new false-conclusion derivations from ganai conditionals plus a nondeterministic stratification guard.
audits.standout: The four-valued QueryResult machinery in logji/src/reasoning.rs (combine_conjunction/combine_disjunction/negate_result/prefer_non_definitive, lines 38-88) paired with the cache rule at reasoning.rs:634-643 that only definitive True/False are tabled: depth exhaustion propagates as ResourceExceeded instead of False through every connective and negation, so fuel/depth limits can never be laundered into negation-as-failure "proofs" — a subtle soundness property most homegrown provers get wrong, here implemented consistently across both evaluators and iterative deepening.
strengths: Four-valued result propagation prevents resource limits and cycle cuts from becoming false NAF negations: ResourceExceeded/Unknown(CycleCut) flow through And/Or/Not/quantifiers and rule-condition loops without collapsing to False, and only definitive results are cached (reasoning.rs:634-643), with cache clear/invalidate verified at every entry point (lib.rs:105,219,305,322,420,583)

Strict tense discrimination is structurally enforced: unify_facts requires the tense wrapper to match (kb.rs:259-267), so bare rule templates never directly match tensed queries; temporal lifting (reasoning.rs:827-971) is the only path and applies the query tense uniformly to all conditions — sound under the timeless-rule reading; probe confirmed bare ganai antecedents gate correctly

The traced query's verdict is computed by the untraced four-valued evaluator (lib.rs:384-399), so the two-valued proof-trace layer cannot corrupt answers, only the displayed derivation; the recent honest-provenance work (EqualitySubstitution with real child derivations, PredicateNotFound instead of unsupported holds:true at reasoning.rs:1106-1171) shows real soundness discipline

Known soundness bugs are honestly filed in todo.md and pinned by failing-pre-fix regression tests (negation-as-failure antecedents, cache poisoning, stacked-poi conjoin), and the xorlo presupposition is correctly suppressed for zero-universal ground conditionals (rules.rs:726-727)

weaknesses.issue: REPRODUCED UNSOUNDNESS: compile_forall_to_rule silently drops antecedent conditions whose template cannot be built — build_rule_template_fact returns None for PastNode/ForAllNode/negated-complex nodes and the condition loop just skips them — so a ganai conditional registers as an UNCONDITIONAL rule. Probes through the full pipeline: (1) 'ganai la .adam. pu bajra gi la .adam. cu tatpi' in an empty KB derives tatpi(adam)=True; (2) 'ganai ro lo gerku cu mlatu gi la .adam. cu gleki' derives gleki(adam)=True though nothing says dogs are cats; (3) 'ganai la .adam. na gerku gi la .adam. cu gleki' plus gerku(adam) derives gleki(adam)=True. Only the poi-na restrictor variant is filed in todo.md; these fire unconditionally and need a fail-closed fix (reject the assertion when any condition atom fails to compile)

REPRODUCED: check_stratification's cycle-negativity test (cycle_has_neg = *is_neg || (has_neg && !dep_neg)) misses negative cycles reached through a negative prefix edge, and DFS roots iterate a HashMap, so the verdict is order-dependent: the unstratifiable program {a←¬b; b←¬c; c←b} was accepted 21/64 runs in a probe. The NAF soundness claim at reasoning.rs:108-111 explicitly relies on this guard; the CycleCut backstop currently prevents false derivations for simple cycles, but identical programs are nondeterministically accepted or rejected across process runs — unacceptable for a deterministic prover

REPRODUCED: negative ground assertions are accepted, assigned fact IDs, and listed by list_facts as active facts, but assert nothing (process_assertion's ground path and collect_ground_facts have no Not handling) — probe: after asserting 'la .adam. gerku' then 'la .adam. na gerku', both appear as active facts while the engine answers gerku(adam)=True and na-gerku=False. For a transparency-focused engine the fact registry misrepresents engine state; belief revision via contrary assertion silently fails instead of erroring

Same fail-open family on the conclusion side: a universal whose consequent atoms cannot be built (e.g. tensed consequent) registers a rule with EMPTY conclusions under the '__fallback__' key that can never fire, while printing '[Rule] Compiled' — silent incompleteness with false success feedback to the user

The traced proof evaluator is two-valued and collapses Unknown/ResourceExceeded to false (.is_true()/.is_false() on four-valued results; traced NotNode negates a bool), so for NAF-over-cycle/depth queries the displayed proof tree can show holds:true 'proof' steps for a verdict the four-valued engine correctly reports as Unknown — the verdict stays sound (taken from the untraced evaluator at lib.rs:386) but trace and verdict can contradict, undermining the Transparency Triad's headline artifact

CountNode 'exactly N' and count_witnesses count du-equivalent constants as distinct individuals (domain members each satisfy via the equivalence-class expansion), so 'exactly 2' can be affirmed in a model with one individual under two names; mitigated because du is unreachable from surface Lojban (only :assert/assert-fact injection). UNPROBED — code-reading finding

Quantifier domain asymmetry: ExistsNode enumerates domain members PLUS all SkolemFn witness terms while ForAllNode enumerates only domain members, so ∀x.P and ∃x.¬P can both report True when a SkolemFn witness falsifies P — the engine can affirm a contradictory pair. UNPROBED — code-reading finding

build_ground_term falls back to GroundTerm::PatternVar for any unsubstituted variable on the ground assertion path, and nothing validates groundness before fact-store insertion (unify_terms will happily bind template vars to a stored PatternVar as if it were a constant); no reachable pipeline path found that triggers it, but it is an unchecked latent corruption vector for the direct assert-fact API

audits.one_line: The WASM host (gasnu) genuinely delivers the Ch16 capability/sandbox claims, but the "production" GraphQL server path and the compute trust boundary do not — adversarial-input bounding (fuel/memory) is absent on the native server path, and the compute oracle can inject arbitrary unverified facts into the zero-hallucination KB.
strengths: WASI ctx is correctly minimized at gasnu/src/main.rs:675 — inherit_stdout().inherit_stderr() only, no stdin/fs preopens/network/env; matches the Ch16 host-isolation claim exactly.

Fuel (consume_fuel + per-command set_fuel refuel) and memory (StoreLimits) are correctly wired for the WASM host (gasnu/src/main.rs:642-689), with friendly [Limit] errors; logji caps reasoning recursion at max_chain_depth=10 in BOTH native and WASM, so pure inference cannot recurse unbounded.

Ambient authority (file :load/:dump/:merge, TCP compute client) lives entirely in the trusted native host, never in the WASM component — the component truly gets no filesystem/network capability, so the sandbox claim is not undermined by host conveniences.

Python reference backend uses a static HANDLERS dict with no eval/exec (no code-injection surface), per-arg type checks, and explicit divide-by-zero / log-domain guards; server has a CORS allowlist (no wildcard), a 1 MiB request body cap, and Prometheus metrics.

weaknesses.issue: The canonical production API server (nibli-server) runs all reasoning through NibliEngine::new() — the native, non-WASM engine — which has NO fuel limit and NO memory limit (only logji's depth-10 cap). The Part IV claim that 'fuel and memory limits bound adversarial input' is false for the very path that handles untrusted HTTP input; a pathological KB/query (e.g. large universal-rule domain enumeration) can consume CPU/RAM unbounded.

Compute-backend trust puncture: when a ComputeNode is evaluated and the external backend returns true, the result is auto-asserted as a PERMANENT ground fact into the KB during the query. The channel (gasnu TCP JSON-Lines) is plaintext and unauthenticated, so a malicious or MITM'd backend injects arbitrary 'facts' into the 'zero-hallucination' store, which downstream rules then chain on. The headline 'every conclusion formally derived' does not hold across the compute boundary, and this trust boundary is undocumented.

request_timeout_middleware wraps next.run in tokio::time::timeout, but the actual reasoning executes inside run_blocking -> task::spawn_blocking. When the timeout fires the client gets a 408, but the spawned blocking thread is detached and runs to completion — server CPU/memory is NOT bounded by the timeout. Repeated expensive queries fill the blocking-thread pool (default cap 512), starving all later requests. The 'request timeouts' hardening protects the client, not the server.

Rate limiter is a single GLOBAL unkeyed bucket (governor RateLimiter::direct), so the default 50 rps cap is shared across ALL clients with no per-IP keying. One client trivially exhausts the global budget and denies every other client (DoS amplifier), and it is in-memory only. For a 'production engineering' claim this is abuse-ineffective.

Schema::build never calls .limit_depth(), .limit_complexity(), or .disable_introspection(), so async-graphql applies NO query depth or complexity bound and introspection is always enabled. Only the 1 MiB body limit constrains a malicious query; schema is fully disclosed even on non-loopback binds (playground also defaults on for loopback).

Python reference backend handle_tenfa computes x2 ** x3 with an unbounded integer exponent before math.isclose. JSON integers parse as Python bigints, so a single request like tenfa with base 3, exponent 2e8 burns CPU/RAM unbounded (measured: 3**2e7 = 4.0s and a 32M-bit integer; cost scales superlinearly). Unauthenticated, and reachable over the network via the documented --host 0.0.0.0. dugri/log are bounded but tenfa is not.

Python backend spawns one unbounded daemon thread per accepted connection with no max-connection / max-thread cap (listen backlog 5 only). A connection flood causes thread exhaustion / memory growth — a trivial unauthenticated DoS, again network-exposed under --host 0.0.0.0.

No CatchPanicLayer on the axum app, and run_blocking does .expect('blocking server task should not panic'). A panic in reasoning on adversarial Lojban (e.g. the unreachable! in compute.rs:142, or arithmetic edge cases) propagates as a JoinError re-panic and aborts the client connection instead of returning a clean 500 — undermining the 'host is protected' robustness posture for the server surface.

audits.standout: The WASM host capability minimization in gasnu: WasiCtxBuilder with only inherit_stdout()/inherit_stderr() plus correctly-wired fuel (consume_fuel + per-command set_fuel refuel) and StoreLimits memory caps — this part genuinely delivers the book's Ch16 host-protection claim, and all ambient authority (fs, TCP) is kept in the host rather than leaking into the component.

audits.one_line: Nibli's reasoning hot paths sit on sound core data structures (predicate-indexed fact store, conclusion-indexed rules, definitive-only tabling) but carry real waste: a dead argument-position index cloned on every insert, eager O(M^k) candidate materialization per backward-chain call, 2-4x redundant evaluation on the traced path the UI exclusively uses, an O(n) rebuild that fires on effectively every Lojban retraction, and a benchmark suite whose query/witness numbers measure structurally unmatchable failure paths.
audits.standout: The predicate-result cache discipline in logji: only definitive True/False results are cached (reasoning.rs:634-643 documents exactly why caching depth/cycle-dependent results would poison sibling branches), the cache is cleared once per query then deliberately persists across iterative-deepening passes for cross-depth tabling (lib.rs:289, 305, kb.rs:860-876), and it is invalidated on every KB mutation — a subtle soundness/performance trade handled precisely right after a previously filed cache-poisoning bug.
strengths: Core indexes are right: InMemoryFactStore keeps a relation->HashSet predicate index giving O(1) contains/lookup (fact_store.rs:72-91), and universal rules are indexed by conclusion predicate in a HashMap of Arc-wrapped records (kb.rs:456, rules.rs:284-291) — goal resolution never linearly scans all rules, and KB snapshots for hypotheticals share rule records via Arc.

Allocation discipline in the untraced core: Cow-based substitute_term avoids cloning ground terms (kb.rs:349-379), with_sub saves/restores a single binding instead of cloning the substitution map per candidate (reasoning.rs:6-23), and cartesian products are lazy iterators rather than materialized sets.

Cross-boundary data is flat and copy-cheap by design: bumpalo arena AST in gerna (freed in one shot per parse), u32-index LogicBuffer/AstBuffer across the WASM boundary, and the smuni flattener expands Biconditional/Xor by sharing subtree indices instead of deep-cloning operands (smuni/src/lib.rs:164-193).

gasnu's production-engineering layer adds no measurable per-command overhead: refuel is a single store.set_fuel call per REPL command and per :load line (gasnu/src/main.rs:608-609), and memory limits use Wasmtime StoreLimits rather than any per-operation accounting.

weaknesses.issue: Entailment-path existential search has no candidate narrowing and backward chaining eagerly materializes all candidates per call: check_formula_holds ExistsNode scans every domain member plus full SkolemFn cartesian products (O(M^dep_count) per registry entry) on every quantifier miss (reasoning.rs:150-178), and build_all_candidates is allocated at the top of EVERY try_backward_chain_typed/traced invocation even when the predicate has zero rules or no unbound event vars (reasoning.rs:676, 1182). The index-driven collect_candidates optimization exists but is wired only into find_witnesses (reasoning.rs:334-359), not entailment. Since smuni wraps every sentence in event-variable existentials, every query pays this; it compounds multiplicatively under recursion and is a plausible mechanism for the >200s universal-rule query hang already filed in todo.md.

arg_position_index is maintained but never read on any query path — confirmed: written on every insert with one full StoredFact clone per argument position plus a relation String clone per position (rules.rs:453-461), removed on incremental retraction (lib.rs:189-195), deep-cloned on every with_assumptions KB snapshot (kb.rs:528), but its only readers are unit tests (tests.rs:5506+); extract_from_index linearly scans lookup_predicate instead (kb.rs:1187-1232). Quantified cost: ~arity x memory duplication of the whole fact store plus per-insert clone overhead for zero benefit. Additional defect: re-asserting an identical fact appends duplicate clones to the index Vec each time (HashSet dedups the store, the Vec does not), so memory grows unboundedly under repeated assertion of the same fact.
