The traced query path — the only path the Transparency Triad UI and REPL '?' use — evaluates everything 2-4x: run_entailment_check_with_proof runs both the untraced and the traced checker per root (lib.rs:384-399, traced result's holds discarded), traced ExistsNode runs a full untraced body check per candidate member then re-runs the traced checker for the winner (reasoning.rs:1680-1692), and each traced rule condition is checked via check_predicate_in_kb_typed and then re-derived by trace_predicate_provenance_typed to build children (reasoning.rs:1284-1306) — all multiplied by iterative-deepening passes that rebuild and discard the full ProofStep vector on every depth-exceeded pass (lib.rs:426-434).

Retraction's incremental fast path is unreachable for any Lojban-asserted fact: Neo-Davidsonian event decomposition puts an ExistsNode into every parsed sentence buffer, so the has_skolems check always routes to full rebuild (lib.rs:160-170), which deep-clones every non-retracted LogicBuffer in the registry and replays process_assertion for each (lib.rs:249-263) — O(total KB) work and allocation per retraction in practice. The incremental path only ever serves assert_fact_direct facts. The stale-derivation/hang aspect is filed in todo.md, but the practical deadness of the incremental path is an unfiled amplification.

The criterion bench suite the book cites does not measure what its names claim: bench_query_latency and bench_witness_extraction populate flat arity-2 gerku(entN,_) facts via assert_fact_direct but query through the event-decomposing Lojban pipeline (exists e. gerku(e) AND gerku_x1(e,adam)) — structurally unmatchable shapes, so both time pure failure-path scans, never a successful lookup (engine_bench.rs:43-55, 84-96); bench_equality asserts a new fact inside b.iter so fact_registry and arg_position_index grow across iterations, biasing timings (engine_bench.rs:128-138); bench_retraction retracts only a direct ground fact, exercising the incremental path real sentences never take, not the O(n) rebuild (engine_bench.rs:146-163); the traced proof path (the UI default) and corpus-scale loads (gdpr/drug-interactions) are entirely unmeasured.

du-equivalence handling performs cartesian-product expansion over equivalence classes twice per failed predicate check: typed_fact_is_asserted_with_equivalence enumerates all argument-class combinations (reasoning.rs:442-474), and after backward chaining fails the fallback enumerates the same product again, this time recursing into check_predicate_in_kb_typed per variant (reasoning.rs:594-621). For an arity-2 fact with a du-chain of n entities this is O(n^2) store probes plus O(n^2) recursive chain attempts per miss, repeated at every recursion level where the predicate misses.

Per-call rework that belongs at registration time: collect_matching_rules_typed clones the Vec<Arc> and re-sorts by priority on every single predicate check (reasoning.rs:529-537) when sort order could be maintained on insert/priority-change; trigger_forward_rules flat-iterates every rule bucket on every fact insert even when zero forward rules exist (rules.rs:489-498) — a cached forward-rule count would skip it; and rules with no conclusions are indexed under a '__fallback__' key that no query path ever consults (rules.rs:292-298), making them dead weight.

String-formatting and duplicate storage on hot paths: trace_predicate_provenance_typed keys its memo on fact.to_display_string(), allocating and formatting the full fact on every provenance call (hit or miss) even though StoredFact already implements Hash/Eq (reasoning.rs:1072-1074); InMemoryFactStore stores every fact twice — once in the facts HashSet and once cloned into the predicate_index HashSet (fact_store.rs:84-91) — so combined with the dead arg_position_index a typical arity-2 fact occupies ~4 heap copies.

audits.one_line: The native-path suite (596 tests across gerna/smuni/logji/nibli-engine) is unusually disciplined for a single-author project — real negative controls, structured proof assertions, fail-before-fix regression pinning — but the canonical WASM production runtime (lasna component + gasnu fuel/memory/sandbox enforcement) has zero automated execution, and the known assert-clean-but-never-fires unsoundness class has no pinning tests, so the suite cannot detect the exact failure mode the book's zero-hallucination claim forbids.
strengths: logji's 232-test suite asserts on structured ProofRule enum variants and children indices (logji/src/tests.rs:1866-2126), not rendered strings — proof-trace tests are robust to formatting churn, and NAF is verified both traced and untraced plus stratified-negation and safe-recursion cases (test_naf_*, test_safe_stratified_negation)

Integration tests use genuinely discriminating negative controls, not just happy paths: ddi checks intermediate mechanism steps (zenba, ckape) so a mechanism regression is caught before the final verdict (nibli-engine/tests/integration.rs:711-743), and gdpr belief revision asserts the naf_dependent JSON provenance flag, not just the boolean flip (integration.rs:517-531)

Regression discipline is real: every audit soundness fix shipped with a fail-before-fix test — stacked_poi_conjoins_both_clauses (integration.rs:641-664) pins the exact pre-fix bug with the cinla-only control that the buggy code wrongly derived

gasnu's compute-backend client is tested against a real mock TCP server including reconnect-after-drop and fractional-arithmetic local-routing edge cases (gasnu/src/main.rs:1355+), and gerna error recovery has dedicated skip/all-fail/line:column/display tests (gerna/src/pipeline_tests.rs)

weaknesses.issue: The canonical production runtime is never executed by any automated test: CI (`just ci`, Justfile:160) never builds the lasna WASM component, nibli-engine integration tests explicitly bypass WASM (integration.rs:4 'No WASM'), and the Part IV production claims — fuel exhaustion/refuel, memory-cap trap, WASI sandbox — are 'tested' only by compile-only smoke tests whose own comments say 'If it compiles and runs, the limits field is present' (gasnu/src/main.rs:1517-1521, 1558-1572, 1575-1595) plus error-message string formatting. No test ever drives a real component to fuel exhaustion or a memory trap.

Known unsoundness classes filed in todo.md have zero pinning tests (no #[ignore] markers anywhere in the workspace): binary-antecedent universal rules compile but silently never fire (todo.md:58, logji/src/reasoning.rs ~687-936), retract+rebuild leaves stale derivations or hangs >200s (todo.md:59), and the replay path escapes rule_source_map (todo.md:82, logji/src/lib.rs:111-127). A rule that asserts cleanly but never fires is invisible to the entire suite — the exact silent-failure mode a zero-hallucination engine must surface.

gdpr.lojban's reasoning behavior is unpinned: gdpr_file_loads_clean only checks lines assert (integration.rs:472-484), and every gdpr_* reasoning test rebuilds a mini-KB inline instead of querying the loaded corpus — so a corpus edit that still parses but stops deriving (the known never-fires class) keeps all tests green while Chapter 20's narrated output breaks. ddi does this right for headline/witness/count but its belief-revision test also rebuilds inline (integration.rs:806-861).

Count quantifier lacks the discriminating overcount control: test_count_exact_match (2 dogs, expect 2) and test_count_mismatch (1 dog, expect 2) both still pass if the implementation's `==` (reasoning.rs:229) regressed to `>=`; there is no N+1 case, no end-to-end `re lo` assert/query test anywhere (only a gerna parse test, pipeline_tests.rs:430), and ExactCountLe is tested only as smuni IR shape.

Fuzz targets are shallow crash-only oracles with no seed corpus and no dictionary: random bytes essentially never pass the gerna morphology gate, so fuzz_assert/fuzz_query exercise only lexer/parser error paths; fuzz_query's blind byte-split (fuzz_query.rs:11) makes a valid assert+query pair vanishingly rare; and no property is checked (assert-then-query-same-text must be True, traced/untraced must agree) despite these being cheap, high-value soundness invariants.

The documented --test-threads=1 requirement is stale: CLAUDE.md and Justfile:67 claim logji needs it for 'shared global state via RefCell', but all shared state is thread_local (compute.rs:9-11, kb.rs:851-854, reasoning.rs:419-422) and the full 232-test logji suite passes green under default parallelism (verified). Meanwhile the real residual hazard — TYPED_PRED_CACHE keyed by StoredFact with no KB identity, safe only because every top-level query path remembers to clear first (lib.rs:305,322,420) — is protected by convention with no test pinning that invariant.

Untraced and traced query paths (check_formula_holds vs check_formula_holds_traced) are duplicated implementations with a documented history of divergence (the AND short-circuit fix), yet their agreement is tested at exactly one point — one KB, one query (query_holds_matches_proof_query_boolean, integration.rs:300-314). No metamorphic pass asserts agreement across the existing test matrix, so the next traced/untraced divergence ships silently.

logji's injectable external-compute dispatch is never tested with a registered mock — register_compute_dispatch (compute.rs:15) has no test call sites, so EVAL_FN is always None in the suite and the external dispatch -> result auto-ingestion path is covered only manually; relatedly, lasna's 1090-line session layer (error-variant mapping, parse-warning surfacing, compute host-bridge) carries only 13 tests, all about go'i snapshots.

audits.standout: ddi_toxicity_requires_both_conditions (nibli-engine/tests/integration.rs:761-796): two purpose-built negative controls — a wide-margin drug whose concentration rises, and a narrow-index drug with no interaction — that jointly discriminate the conjunction gate, with an inline comment identifying exactly which control fails if the toxicity step ignores a premise. This is mutation-testing thinking done by hand, and it is the pattern the rest of the suite should be held to.

architecture.assessment:
The macro decomposition is genuinely sound: gerna/smuni/logji/lasna map onto real seams (parse / semantics / inference / boundary glue), dependency direction is clean (gerna ← smuni ← lasna; logji independent of the front end), and the extraction of nibli-types as a shared leaf crate for AST/logic/error types was the right move to break what would otherwise be a WIT-mirror duplication knot. wit/world.wit is the strongest artifact in the codebase — flat u32-indexed buffers used consistently across ast-buffer/logic-buffer/proof-trace, resource-based lifecycles, one shared nibli-error variant, and doc comments good enough to read as a specification. The injectable compute dispatch (function pointers registered by the host, batch API to amortize boundary crossings) is a clean solution to the cfg-gated-import problem, and the four-valued QueryResult living in the shared types crate makes 'resource exhaustion never masquerades as False' an architectural property rather than a local convention.

But the architecture carries three structural liabilities that are already producing user-visible divergence, which is exactly what a production-aspiring claim cannot afford. First, there are two parallel pipeline-glue implementations — lasna's compile_pipeline (lasna/src/lib.rs:593-636) for the WASM/REPL surface and nibli-engine's compile_text (nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:334-371) for the server/UI surface — and they have drifted: go'i/observative resolution exists only in lasna, and lasna's query paths silently evaluate partial ASTs when sentences fail to parse while nibli-engine aborts. The same Lojban input means different things on the two canonical runtime surfaces; nibli-engine should be the single orchestrator with lasna a thin WIT adapter over it. Second, the logic IR exists in seven representations (smuni tree IR, nibli-types flat IR, WIT guest + host bindings, nibli-store, nibli-protocol JSON, logji GroundTerm) bridged by six hand-written conversion lattices; exhaustive matches make pairwise drift compile-visible, but adding one IR variant touches ~8 sites across 6 crates plus three evaluators — the dominant friction the next contributor will hit. Third, logji contains three parallel recursive interpreters (untraced, traced, witness-finding) that must agree by discipline alone; the orchestration layer literally runs both untraced and traced evaluators per query and discards the traced verdict (let _ = holds; logji/src/lib.rs:399), so the proof displayed and the answer returned can diverge — and per the reasoning-unit probes, they do. For an engine whose entire thesis is that the proof IS the answer, dual evaluation without a consistency check is the single most consequential architectural flaw.

Secondary debt: the per-stage component architecture the book describes is vestigial (only the monolithic lasna-pipeline world is deployed, so the flat-buffer interchange pays its tax across no actual boundary); logji harbors thread-local global state (predicate cache, compute dispatch fn-pointers) directly contradicting its documented 'no global state' model, with multithreaded-server correctness resting on a clear-at-every-query discipline enforced two crates away; and the typed error contract evaporates in practice — logji assigns NibliError categories by call-site rather than origin, nibli-engine erases the variant to String on every method, and recovery paths (rebuild replay, persistent replay, retraction persistence) silently drop errors. Module hygiene is middling: glob re-exports make logji's five files one flat namespace, and reasoning.rs (2054 lines), gasnu/main.rs (1926 lines), and a 6041-line single test file concentrate risk.

Net: above-average bones with a genuinely excellent boundary contract, undermined by duplication-driven semantic drift on the two surfaces users actually touch and a no-cross-check dual-evaluator core. A focused consolidation (single glue crate, evaluator unification or a verdict-consistency assertion, IR-representation diet) would lift this to solidly professional; as it stands it is one notch below.
architecture_issues: MAJOR: Duplicated pipeline orchestration with semantic drift between the two canonical surfaces — lasna/src/lib.rs:593-636 (compile_pipeline) vs nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:334-371 (compile_text): go'i/SelbriSnapshot resolution exists only in lasna (27 refs vs 0), and parse-error policy diverges (lasna query paths discard parse warnings and evaluate the partial AST at lib.rs:682/697/710/726; nibli-engine aborts on any parse error). Same Lojban input behaves differently in REPL vs server/UI.

MAJOR: Triple-evaluator duplication in logji with no consistency enforcement — check_formula_holds, check_formula_holds_traced, and find_witnesses are parallel interpreters of the same LogicBuffer semantics; the traced query path runs both evaluators and discards the traced verdict (let _ = holds; logji/src/lib.rs:399), so the returned answer and the displayed proof can diverge (and per unit probes, do), while paying 2x evaluation per iterative-deepening pass on the path the UI exclusively uses.

MODERATE-MAJOR: Seven parallel representations of the logic IR (smuni::ir::LogicalForm, nibli-types::logic::LogicNode, WIT guest bindings in lasna, WIT host bindings in gasnu, nibli-store::StoredLogicNode, nibli-protocol JSON types, logji::kb::GroundTerm) bridged by hand-written conversion lattices (lasna/src/lib.rs:57-198, nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:33-236); adding one IR variant requires edits in ~8 sites across 6 crates plus three logji evaluators — the dominant change-amplification cost for any contributor.

MODERATE: Thread-local global state in logji contradicts its documented architecture ('no global state', logji/src/lib.rs:18) — the predicate result cache (logji/src/kb.rs:851-877) and compute dispatch registration as plain fn-pointer thread-locals (logji/src/compute.rs:6-18); multi-KB-per-thread correctness rests on clear-at-every-query discipline enforced in a different crate, and the fn-pointer thread-local makes external compute structurally unregistrable on a multithreaded server (every tokio worker sees None), foreclosing the compute feature on the canonical API surface.

MODERATE: The error-type strategy collapses at every boundary it was designed for — logji assigns NibliError categories by call-site not origin (assert_fact maps all failures to Semantic, logji/src/lib.rs:482-484), nibli-engine erases the typed variant to String on all public methods (nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:385/432/446 etc.) so server/UI cannot pattern-match error classes, and recovery paths silently drop errors (rebuild replay ignores per-fact failures logji/src/lib.rs:261; persistent replay ignores assert results nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:313-314; retraction never written to the persistent store nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:489-491). Also max_chain_depth is mutated as loop state with no restore on the ? error path (logji/src/lib.rs:308-316, 426-435).

MODERATE: The per-stage component architecture is vestigial — only the monolithic lasna-pipeline world ships; gerna-component/smuni-component/logji-component worlds (wit/world.wit:474-482) and their binding-stub structs are never deployed, so the flat AstBuffer/LogicBuffer interchange crosses no production component boundary while still imposing flatten/re-interpret cost and constraining the IR; the retained legacy worlds mislead contributors about where the real boundaries are.

praise_themes: Flat u32-indexed buffer architecture (AstBuffer/LogicBuffer) across the WASM boundary with exhaustive, compiler-enforced match conversions — independently praised by the gerna, smuni, WIT, and lasna reviewers as the right component-interop design.

Genuine regression discipline: every shipped soundness fix is pinned by a test that failed pre-fix with the exact mechanism documented, and integration tests use discriminating negative controls (apixaban/CYP3A4, conjunction gates, three-way matrices) rather than happy paths.

Four-valued QueryResult lattice with definitive-only tabling and consistent cache clearing/invalidation — resource limits and cycle cuts cannot silently collapse into false NAF negations, and the authoritative verdict always comes from the four-valued evaluator.

Fail-closed instincts where implemented: the implicit-ke'a ambiguity firewall refuses to guess, provenance is honest (EqualitySubstitution/PredicateNotFound instead of bogus holds:true), and naf_dependent is surfaced and test-pinned in proof JSON.

gasnu host hardening is genuinely wired and unusually well tested for a REPL binary: minimal WASI ctx (stdout/stderr only), per-command refuel, live-updatable memory limiter, and a mock-TCP-tested compute client including reconnect-after-drop.

Specification-grade documentation and candid limitation tracking: doc-commented WIT/AST/KB fields throughout, intention-revealing TODO comments, and an honest todo.md that downgrades its own findings when verification warrants.

confirmed.title: Universal-rule compilation fails open: unrepresentable antecedent atoms silently dropped, registering unconditional rules
confirmed.detail: build_rule_template_fact(_with_negation) returns None for Or/PastNode/ForAllNode/negated-complex condition atoms and the loop at logji/src/rules.rs:677-685 silently skips them, so 'ganai ga A gi B gi C' registers a ZERO-condition rule — probe-confirmed: consequent derives True in an empty KB. Variants also reproduced: tensed antecedent ('pu bajra') and nested-universal antecedent fire unconditionally; rules.rs:586-592 additionally strips tense wrappers when collecting universals so a past-tensed universal fires for any tense. Same registration layer: check_stratification (rules.rs:351-378) loses negative-edge position and iterates HashMap-ordered DFS roots — the unstratifiable program {a<-not b; b<-not c; c<-b} was accepted 21/64 process runs (nondeterministic NAF guard). todo.md files only the 'poi na' negated-existential restrictor (line 60) and negated-conclusion/__fallback__ (line 61) variants; the unconditional-firing class and stratification nondeterminism are unfiled. Fix direction: fail closed — reject the assertion when any condition atom fails to compile.
sources: logji universal rule compilation + session layer
confirmed.check_hint: Full pipeline, empty KB: assert 'ganai ga <A> gi <B> gi <C>' Lojban with neither disjunct asserted, query the consequent (expect wrong True); repeat with 'ganai la .adam. pu bajra gi la .adam. cu tatpi'. For stratification: register the 3-rule NAF program in ~50 fresh processes and count accept vs reject.

adjudication.evidence: All three parts reproduced. (1) Fail-open condition compilation: build_rule_template_fact (logji/src/rules.rs:1001-1050) returns Some only for Predicate/ComputeNode/skolemized-Exists; the `_ => None` catch-all at rules.rs:1048 covers Or/And/Not/ForAll and ALL tense wrappers (no Past/Future arm, unlike build_stored_fact_from_node at rules.rs:917-928). The condition loop at rules.rs:677-686 silently skips None atoms and register_rule proceeds with empty typed_conds (rules.rs:710-718). Backward chaining fires zero-condition rules vacuously (reasoning.rs:1253 `.all()` over empty list; condition loop at 1284 skipped; the comment at reasoning.rs:1279-1280 explicitly endorses childless Derived steps). Routing: kb.rs:901-912 sends ground Or(Not(P),Q) through compile_forall_to_rule, and kb.rs:890-896 plus rules.rs:586-595 strip tense wrappers en route. PROBE (full pipeline via nibli-engine, temp test, since deleted): on an EMPTY KB, asserting `ganai ga la .adam. cu bajra gi la .adam. cu cadzu gi la .adam. cu tatpi` makes `query_holds("la .adam. cu tatpi")` return True; tensed antecedent `ganai la .adam. pu bajra gi ...` -> True; nested-universal antecedent `ganai ro lo gerku cu danlu gi ...` -> True. Controls isolate the cause: plain antecedent `ganai la .adam. cu bajra gi ...` correctly gives False on empty KB and True after asserting the antecedent. (2) Stratification: rules.rs:352 `cycle_has_neg = *is_neg || (has_neg && !dep_neg)` tracks path-from-DFS-root negativity, so a negative edge entering the cycle from outside (a->(b,neg)) sets neg_on_path[b]=true and masks the in-cycle negative edge b->(c,neg); DFS roots iterate HashMap graph.keys() (rules.rs:374). A verbatim copy of check_stratification run on {a->[(b,neg)], b->[(c,neg)], c->[(b,pos)]} across 64 fresh processes: 19 ACCEPTED / 45 REJECTED, accepted exactly when key order starts at "a" — matching the reported 21/64 (~1/3). Caveat: this probe is at the function level (check_stratification is private); pure-surface-Lojban construction of that exact NAF cycle may be obstructed by the same condition-dropping bugs, but the guard protects register_rule for all rule paths and is demonstrably order-dependent. (3) Unfiled: todo.md line 60 covers only the `poi na` negated-existential restrictor drop and line 61 the negated-CONCLUSION/__fallback__ + bare-universal cases (line 61 even names the `_ => None` catch-all but only conclusion-side); no todo.md item covers condition-side unconditional firing, and "strat" appears nowhere in todo.md.
adjudication.fair_wording: Universal/conditional rule compilation in logji fails open: condition atoms that the template builder cannot represent (disjunctions, tensed antecedents, nested universals, negated complex bodies — anything hitting build_rule_template_fact's `_ => None` at rules.rs:1048) are silently dropped by the loop at rules.rs:677-686, and the rule is registered anyway, possibly with zero conditions. Probe-confirmed end-to-end on an empty KB: `ganai ga A gi B gi C`, `ganai ... pu bajra gi ...`, and `ganai ro lo gerku cu danlu gi ...` each derive their consequent True with no supporting facts — a major unsoundness in a system whose core claim is zero-hallucination ("every conclusion is formally derived"). Compounding this, the spine walks at rules.rs:586-595 and kb.rs:890-896 discard tense wrappers during rule collection. Separately, the NAF stratification guard (check_stratification, rules.rs:321-380) is unreliable: line 352 loses the position of negative edges (an incoming negative edge from outside a cycle masks an in-cycle one) and roots iterate HashMap order (line 374), so the unstratifiable program {a<-not b; b<-not c; c<-b} is accepted ~1/3 of process runs nondeterministically (probe: 19/64 accepted at function level; surface-Lojban reachability of that exact program not established). todo.md files only the conclusion-side and `poi na` relatives of this bug class (lines 60-61); the condition-side fail-open class and the stratification defects are new, unfiled findings. Fix direction is sound: fail closed — reject the assertion (or refuse to register the rule) when any antecedent atom fails to compile, and make stratification root-order-independent while tracking in-cycle negative edges precisely.

confirmed.title: Assertions can silently ingest nothing yet return success with a fact id (Xor, bare disjunctions, negated ground facts)
confirmed.detail: Xor expansion And(Or(A,B), Not(And(A,B))) from '.i ju' is unrepresentable by logji's assertion path: collect_ground_facts skips Or-without-Not and Not nodes and register_ground_material_conditional matches neither conjunct, while smuni's eager expansion (smuni/src/lib.rs:180-193) erases the Xor variant at flattening so downstream cannot reject. Probe-confirmed: assert succeeds with a fact id, zero facts/rules ingested, and querying the identical sentence back returns False. Bare '.i ja' disjunctive assertions share the fate. Same family (Soundness audit, probe-confirmed): negative ground assertions ('la .adam. na gerku') are accepted, listed as active facts by list_facts, and assert nothing (logji/src/kb.rs:991-1022, rules.rs:935-972) — the registry misrepresents engine state and contrary-assertion belief revision silently fails. Breaks the assert-then-query-same-text invariant. Not filed in todo.md.
confirmed.check_hint: Assert 'A .i ju B', 'A .i ja B', and 'la .adam. na gerku' (after asserting the positive); query each identical sentence back and diff list_facts against the actual fact store contents.

adjudication.evidence:
CODE PATH (all cited lines verified by reading): (1) smuni/src/lib.rs:180-193 expands Xor ('.i ju') to And(Or(A,B), Not(And(A,B))) at flattening, erasing the Xor variant before logji sees it — downstream cannot detect/reject it. (2) logji/src/rules.rs:930 — build_stored_fact_from_node returns None for And/Or/Not/ForAll/Count; rules.rs:935-972 — collect_ground_facts only descends And/Exists/tense/deontic wrappers, so Or and Not subtrees yield zero leaf facts. (3) logji/src/kb.rs:878-928 — register_ground_material_conditional registers rules only for Or(Not(P),Q) (kb.rs:905) or Or(Q,Not(P)) (kb.rs:916); bare Or(A,B) with positive event-decomposed operands (Exists children) matches neither branch, and a top-level Not falls to the catch-all `_ => {}` (kb.rs:925). (4) logji/src/lib.rs:90-107 — assert_fact_inner unconditionally inserts a FactRecord and returns Ok(id) with no zero-ingestion check; lib.rs:268-282 — list_facts_inner reports registry entries, not actual ingested state. (5) logji/src/fact_store.rs — StoredFact storage has no negative-fact representation at all.

LIVE PROBE (temp test nibli-engine/tests/probe_finding_tmp.rs, run via cargo test, then deleted; git status restored): CONTROL: assert 'la .adam. gerku' → id 0, query-back True (invariant normally holds). XOR: assert 'la .adam. gerku .i ju la .adam. mlatu' → Ok(0), list_facts shows 1 active fact, query of the identical sentence back → False, left disjunct → False. JA: assert 'la .adam. gerku .i ja la .adam. mlatu' → Ok(0), list_facts 1, query-back → False. NEGATED-AFTER-POSITIVE: assert positive (id 0), assert 'la .adam. na gerku' → Ok(1); list_facts shows BOTH entries active [(0,'la .adam. gerku'),(1,'la .adam. na gerku')]; query 'la .adam. na gerku' → False; query positive → True; check_contradictions() → [] (the contrary assertion is not even flagged as a contradiction). NEGATED-ALONE: assert Ok(0), query-back → True — but only vacuously via negation-as-failure (nothing stored; an empty KB makes Not(P) true).

NOT FILED: grepped todo.md — no item covers Xor/bare-disjunction assertion or negated ground assertion no-ops. Item 60 (negated poi-na restrictor dropped in universal-rule antecedents, rules.rs) is a different, rule-compilation-level bug. Note one scoping nuance the finding already got right: '.i jo' biconditional is NOT affected (both expanded directions are Or(Not,·) and register rules via kb.rs:905→compile_forall_to_rule).
adjudication.fair_wording: Confirmed, major (broken contract + silent belief-revision failure). Three assertion shapes that smuni compiles and the pipeline accepts — '.i ju' Xor, bare '.i ja' disjunction, and negated ground facts ('na <selbri>') — pass through logji's assertion path ingesting zero facts and zero rules, yet assert_fact returns success with a fact id, and list_facts reports them as active KB facts. Probe-verified end-to-end: querying the identical sentence back returns False for ju/ja, and a contrary negative assertion after a positive is a complete no-op (negative queries False, positive stays True, check_contradictions reports nothing). The root causes are (a) smuni's flattener (smuni/src/lib.rs:180-193) erasing the Xor variant into And/Or/Not primitives that logji's ground path cannot represent, and (b) logji's collect_ground_facts (rules.rs:935-972, leaf fallback rules.rs:930) and register_ground_material_conditional (kb.rs:878-928) silently skipping Or-without-Not and Not nodes with no error or warning. One softening nuance: a negated ground assertion in an otherwise-empty KB coincidentally query-backs True via negation-as-failure, so the invariant breach surfaces only when a contrary positive exists — but the registry misrepresentation (success + active listing for a no-op) holds in all three cases. For a 'zero-hallucination' production-aspiring engine, accepting an assertion it cannot represent without signaling is precisely the failure class the typed nibli-error contract exists to prevent; minimum fix is to reject (or warn on) assertions whose processing produces zero facts and zero rules. Not filed in todo.md (item 60 is the distinct poi-na rule-antecedent bug).

confirmed.title: smuni silently discards meaning: rel clauses on la/le/pro-sumti dropped, connected sumti under tags lose right operand, overflow FA args vanish
confirmed.detail: The restrictor attach AND the ambiguity-firewall error both sit inside 'if let Some(last) = quants.last_mut()' (smuni/src/semantic/helpers.rs:226), so sumti that introduce no quantifier silently drop the compiled clause: verified 'la .adam. poi gerku cu klama' asserts bare klama(adam), 'le gerku poi barda' loses barda — exactly the silent-drop class the firewall commit claimed eliminated. resolve_sumti's Connected arm resolves only the left child (helpers.rs:276-279): verified 'fa mi .e do klama' loses do, 'mi klama ri'a do .e ti' loses ti (root cause partially filed as todo.md:36 for the second-connected-sumti case; tag/modal/be-bei reachability is unfiled). FA tags beyond predicate arity drop the term with no diagnostic and untagged-after-tagged fills first-free-slot vs CLL's place counter (compile.rs:96-98). Also internally inconsistent: tanru event decomposition skips Unspecified roles (selbri.rs:74,86), causing false firewall rejections of valid 'lo gerku poi sutra bajra cu klama'.
confirmed.check_hint: Compile each probe sentence and inspect the emitted FOL for the dropped conjunct/operand: 'la .adam. poi gerku cu klama', 'le gerku poi barda cu klama', 'fa mi .e do klama', 'mi klama ri'a do .e ti', 'fu do gerku', 'lo gerku poi sutra bajra cu klama'.

adjudication.evidence: All four sub-claims verified by code reading plus runtime probes through the full native pipeline (NibliEngine::compile_debug / query_holds; temp probe test created and deleted). (1) Silent rel-clause drop: in smuni/src/semantic/helpers.rs the Sumti::Restricted arm compiles the clause body (line 219-220) but both the restrictor attach AND the ambiguity-firewall errors sit inside `if let Some(last) = quants.last_mut()` (line 226); inner sumti returning empty quants — la->Constant (helpers.rs:168-174), le->Description (helpers.rs:175-181), ProSumti (101-126), Name (127-130) — discard rel_body with no error. Probe: 'la .adam. poi gerku cu klama' compiled to klama(adam) event decomposition with ZERO gerku predicates and no error; 'le gerku poi barda cu klama' lost barda. Soundness probe: KB containing only 'la .adam. cu klama' answers query 'la .adam. poi gerku cu klama' = True (engine affirms an unsupported restriction). This hole is NOT in todo.md, whose lines 3/7 claim the rel-clause firewall guarantee is closed. (2) Connected sumti under tags: parser wraps tag outside connective (gerna/src/grammar/sumti.rs:22-24 with try_parse_sumti attaching connectives at 112-121), so 'fa mi .e do' = Tagged(Fa, Connected(...)); compile_bridi's distribution scan matches only direct Sumti::Connected (compile.rs:26), so the term falls to resolve_sumti's Connected arm which resolves only left_id (helpers.rs:276-279). Probes: 'fa mi .e do klama' lost do; 'mi klama ri'a do .e ti' compiled to rinka(do,mi) with ti gone; control 'mi .e do klama' correctly produced both conjuncts. Root cause is filed (todo.md item 36, 'second connected sumti'), but the tagged/modal reachability — where the FIRST and only connected sumti loses its right operand — is unfiled. (3) FA overflow: compile.rs:96-98 `if idx < target_arity { positioned[idx] = Some(term); }` has no else; probe 'fu do gerku' (gerku arity 2, fu=slot 5) compiled with do absent and no diagnostic. Untagged-after-tagged first-free-slot fill (compile.rs:113-120) vs CLL's resume-after-tag place counter confirmed statically (not probed). (4) Internal inconsistency: the Tanru arm of apply_selbri skips Unspecified roles (selbri.rs:74,86) while root event_decompose emits all roles (selbri.rs:25-34, the documented contract); count_unspecified_predicates (helpers.rs:284-320) then finds 0 injectable slots. Probe: 'lo gerku poi sutra bajra cu klama' rejected with '[Semantic Error] Relative clause: the described entity has no unambiguous subject (x1) slot...'; control 'lo gerku poi bajra cu klama' compiles and injects correctly — a false rejection of valid Lojban (loud error, not silent drop).
adjudication.fair_wording: smuni silently discards meaning in three confirmed ways and falsely rejects in a fourth. (a) MAJOR, NEW: relative clauses on sumti that introduce no quantifier (la names, le descriptions, pro-sumti) are compiled then silently dropped, because both the restrictor attach and the ambiguity firewall are gated on quants.last_mut() (smuni/src/semantic/helpers.rs:226); probe-verified that a KB knowing only klama(adam) answers 'la .adam. poi gerku cu klama' TRUE — an unsound affirmative for a zero-hallucination engine, and a hole in the firewall todo.md declares closed. (b) MAJOR reachability of a filed bug: a connected sumti wrapped in a place tag or BAI modal ('fa mi .e do klama', 'mi klama ri'a do .e ti') bypasses compile_bridi's Connected distribution (compile.rs:26) and hits resolve_sumti's left-only Connected arm (helpers.rs:276-279), silently dropping the right operand even for the first/only connective — todo.md:36 files this arm only for the second-connected-sumti case. (c) MODERATE, NEW: FA tags beyond predicate arity drop the term with no diagnostic (compile.rs:96-98, probe 'fu do gerku' loses do), and untagged-after-tagged slot filling uses first-free-slot rather than CLL's place counter (compile.rs:113-120). (d) MODERATE, NEW: tanru event decomposition skips Unspecified roles (selbri.rs:74,86), inconsistent with root decomposition, so the firewall falsely rejects valid 'lo gerku poi sutra bajra cu klama' with a semantic error while 'poi bajra' works. (a)-(c) are silent meaning loss in assertions and unsound TRUE answers in queries; (d) is a loud false rejection. One caveat: the CLL place-counter deviation was confirmed by code reading only, and (b)'s root cause is already partially tracked.

confirmed.title: gerna flattener records wrong body indices for Abstraction and Restricted arms (discarded push_sentence return value)
confirmed.detail: gerna/src/lib.rs:244 (Abstraction) and :320 (Restricted) record body_idx = sentences.len() BEFORE recursing instead of using push_sentence's return value (the Connected arm at lib.rs:146-147 does it correctly). Probe-confirmed by two independent reviewers: 'mi djica lo nu lo gerku poi barda cu klama kei' yields an Abstraction whose body resolves to the barda rel-clause bridi; 'lo nu nelci lo nu klama kei kei' points BOTH abstraction nodes at klama with the nelci body orphaned; since abstraction bodies parse via parse_sentence, 'lo nu ganai broda gi brode kei' silently becomes an abstraction over just broda — no nesting required. Silent miscompilation fed to smuni with no error; one-line fix. Flattener tests never assert nested index integrity (flattener_tests.rs:89-155). Not filed in todo.md.
confirmed.check_hint: Flatten 'mi djica lo nu lo gerku poi barda cu klama kei' and 'lo nu nelci lo nu klama kei kei'; compare recorded body indices against push_sentence's actual return values.

adjudication.evidence:
Code: gerna/src/lib.rs:244-245 (Abstraction arm) and :320-321 (Restricted arm) record `body_idx = self.buffer.sentences.len() as u32` BEFORE calling `self.push_sentence(...)` and discard its return value, while the Connected arm at lib.rs:146-147 correctly uses the return values. push_sentence's Simple arm (lib.rs:100-139) pushes its selbri/sumti first — which recursively push any nested abstraction/rel-clause body sentences into buffer.sentences — and only then appends the bridi itself, so the pre-recorded index points at the FIRST nested inner sentence, not the body bridi. Abstraction bodies parse via full parse_sentence (gerna/src/grammar/selbri.rs:165), so Connected bodies and nested clauses are all reachable.

Probe (temp test module compiled and run via `cargo test --lib -p gerna`, all 4 probes confirmed the bug, files deleted after):
1. "mi djica lo nu lo gerku poi barda cu klama kei" → sentences=[barda(0), klama(1), djica(2)]; Abstraction body_idx=0 resolves to the barda rel-clause bridi instead of klama(1).
2. "lo nu nelci lo nu klama kei kei cu xamgu" → BOTH Abstraction nodes record body_idx=0 → klama; the nelci bridi (sentence 1) is orphaned, exactly as reported.
3. "mi djica lo nu ganai broda gi brode kei" → sentences=[broda(0), brode(1), Connected(GanaiGi,0,1)(2), djica(3)]; Abstraction body_idx=0 → broda alone, the conditional silently vanishes — no nesting required.
4. (Broader than the finding's probes) "lo prenu poi djica lo nu klama kei cu xamgu" → Restricted body_sentence=0 resolves to the inner klama abstraction body instead of the djica rel-clause bridi — the Restricted arm misfires symmetrically when a rel-clause body contains a nested abstraction.

All indices are in-range, so smuni consumes the wrong body with no error — silent miscompilation. Test-coverage claim verified: gerna/src/flattener_tests.rs:89-155 only asserts sentences.len() and roots.len(), never which sentence a body index resolves to (and the test fixtures have at most one nested sentence, where len()-before-push happens to be correct). Not filed in todo.md (grep for flatten/body_idx/push_sentence finds only unrelated prenex/rules entries). Fix is one line per arm: `let body_idx = self.push_sentence(...)`. Severity: major — soundness-class silent miscompilation in a "zero-hallucination" prover, triggered by ordinary Lojban (any abstraction over a connected sentence, or any nesting of abstraction/relative-clause inside another's body).
adjudication.fair_wording: The gerna flattener records wrong body indices for Abstraction (gerna/src/lib.rs:244) and Restricted rel-clause (lib.rs:320) nodes: it snapshots sentences.len() before recursing and discards push_sentence's return value, while push_sentence appends child/nested sentences before the body bridi itself. Probe-confirmed in four cases: an abstraction whose body contains a rel clause binds to the rel-clause bridi; nested abstractions both bind to the innermost body, orphaning the outer one; an abstraction over "ganai A gi B" silently becomes an abstraction over just A (no nesting needed); and a rel clause whose body contains an abstraction binds to the abstraction's body. The wrong indices are always in-range, so smuni miscompiles silently. The correct pattern already exists in the same function (Connected arm, lib.rs:146-147); the fix is one line per arm. Flattener tests (flattener_tests.rs:89-155) only assert counts, never index targets, and the bug is not filed in todo.md. Major: silent unsoundness reachable from ordinary surface Lojban in an engine whose core claim is that every conclusion is formally derived.

confirmed.title: Parser front-end silently corrupts input: lexer truncation at first unlexable char, recovery re-pairs connectives across unrelated sentences, depth-guard leak
confirmed.detail: Three probe-confirmed defects violating the error-reporting contract. (1) tokenize's 'while let Some(Ok(token))' stops at the first unlexable character with ZERO errors (gerna/src/lexer.rs:198): 'mi klama 7 do prami .i lo gerku cu danlu' silently drops everything from '7' and asserts the truncated prefix; also kills zoi payloads of non-Lojban text. (2) After a failed sentence, '.i ja B' pops the last SUCCESSFULLY parsed (unrelated) sentence as left operand (grammar.rs:387-404): 'mi klama .i lo .i ja do prami' replaces the valid standalone assertion with Connected(Ja, mi-klama, do-prami), asserted with only a warning; the fallback asserts B standalone even under ja/jonai semantics. (3) enter()/leave() depth counters do not pair on '?' early returns and parse_text never resets depth between recovered sentences (grammar.rs:153-164): 41 malformed sentences then a valid 'mi klama' yields 11 spurious depth errors and the valid sentence FAILS — defeats :load corpus recovery. The headline line:column feature has no test asserting actual positions. Not filed in todo.md.
confirmed.check_hint: Run the three probes verbatim: digit-mid-sentence truncation (expect zero errors, lost content), '.i ja' re-pairing after a failed sentence, and 41-bad-then-valid depth exhaustion.

adjudication.evidence: All three defects probe-confirmed (cargo example against gerna rlib, rustc 1.94.0; probe file deleted after use). (1) LEXER TRUNCATION — gerna/src/lexer.rs:198 `while let Some(Ok(token)) = lex.next()` terminates at the first logos Err; tokenize() returns Vec with no error channel. Probe: "mi klama 7 do prami .i lo gerku cu danlu" → 2 tokens ["mi","klama"], parses as Simple(klama, [mi]) with 0 errors — everything from '7' silently dropped. grammar.rs:433 "unconsumed tokens remaining" cannot fire (tokens never produced). zoi probe: "mi cusku zoi gy. abc123 .gy" → QuotedLiteral(". abc"), 0 errors (payload corrupted at the digit). lasna/src/lib.rs:609-636 errors only when ast.roots is empty, so the truncated sentence is asserted into the KB with zero warnings — silent data corruption in a zero-hallucination engine. (2) CONNECTIVE RE-PAIRING — grammar.rs:387-404: after a failed sentence (error + skip_to_next_dot_i), `.i ja B` pops the last SUCCESSFULLY parsed sentence (grammar.rs:395 sentences.pop()) regardless of the intervening failure. Probe: "mi klama .i lo .i ja do prami" → 1 sentence Connected(Ja, mi-klama, do-prami), 1 error; the valid standalone assertion 'mi klama' is replaced by a strictly weaker disjunction with an operand the author never paired. Fallback grammar.rs:401-404 asserts B standalone even for ja/jonai (A∨B ⊬ B): probe "lo .i ja do prami" → asserts 'do prami' alone. Only surfaced as a joinable warning via lasna; assertion proceeds. (3) DEPTH-GUARD LEAK — enter() (grammar.rs:153-160) increments before checking and returns Err without decrement; sentence.rs:51,60,70,93 propagate errors via `?` without leave(); parse_text never resets self.depth across recovered sentences. Probe: 41 × "ganai lo .i " + "mi klama" → sentences=0 (valid sentence FAILS), 42 errors, 11 spurious "maximum nesting depth exceeded" — the finding's exact numbers (tipping point n=32; each ganai-lo failure leaks 2 frames). Caveat: simplest bad shape ("lo") leaks 1 frame/failure, so 41 of those do NOT trip it (probe: 0 depth errors); and REPL :load parses per line with a fresh Parser, so depth resets between lines — the leak defeats recovery within a single multi-sentence parse_text call, not across :load lines. SUB-CLAIM CORRECTIONS: "no test asserting actual positions" is overstated — gerna/src/pipeline_tests.rs:536-552 asserts exact LINE numbers (line==2, line==1); only exact COLUMN values are never asserted (column>0 only). "Not filed in todo.md" verified: todo.md's gerna section files prenex/du/y-vowel/gismu-cluster/dangling-connective/gonai items, none of these three.
adjudication.fair_wording: Parser front-end has three probe-confirmed defects that silently corrupt asserted knowledge, violating the error-reporting contract of a zero-hallucination engine; none is filed in todo.md. (1) MAJOR: tokenize() (gerna/src/lexer.rs:198) stops at the first unlexable character and discards the rest of the input with zero errors — "mi klama 7 do prami .i lo gerku cu danlu" asserts the truncated prefix "mi klama" as fact with no warning (also corrupts zoi payloads containing non-Lojban characters such as digits). (2) MAJOR: after a failed sentence, an ".i ja/je/jo/ju" connective re-pairs with the last successfully parsed, unrelated sentence (grammar.rs:395), destroying a valid standalone assertion by replacing it with a connective formula the author never wrote ("mi klama .i lo .i ja do prami" → Connected(Ja, mi-klama, do-prami)); when no left sentence exists the right operand is asserted standalone even under disjunctive (ja/jonai) semantics — both asserted with only a warning. (3) MODERATE: recursion-depth counters leak on error paths (enter() at grammar.rs:153-160 increments without rollback; `?` early returns at grammar/sentence.rs:51,60,70,93 skip leave()), and parse_text never resets depth between recovered sentences — 41 malformed "ganai lo" sentences then a valid "mi klama" yields 11 spurious "maximum nesting depth exceeded" errors and the valid sentence fails to parse. Scope corrections vs the original report: the depth leak requires malformed sentences that fail ≥2 frames deep (the simplest shape leaks 1 frame/failure and 41 of those do not trip it); REPL :load parses each line with a fresh parser, so the leak poisons recovery only within a single multi-sentence input (e.g. one long line or the UI tab text), not across :load lines; and exact error LINE numbers are test-asserted (pipeline_tests.rs:536-552) — only exact column values are untested.

confirmed.title: Retraction/registry unsoundness: duplicate ground facts conflated, only roots.first() retracted, non-atomic assertion, rebuild swallows errors
confirmed.detail: Probe-confirmed: the HashSet fact store tracks no multiplicity, so retracting one of two registry records asserting the same ground fact (reachable via :assert / assert-fact) removes the fact entirely — query returns False while list_facts shows an active record still asserting it; incremental and rebuild paths disagree (logji/src/lib.rs:187). Probe-confirmed: incremental retraction collects leaves only from roots.first() while assertion processes ALL roots — later-root facts of a multi-root buffer survive retraction (post-retraction hallucination, lib.rs:179). Compounding: a mid-way process_assertion failure leaves earlier roots' facts/rules in the KB with no FactRecord — orphaned, unretractable state (lib.rs:90-107); rebuild_inner swallows per-buffer replay errors with 'let _ =' and clears predicate_registry, silently wiping user-declared arg_sorts (lib.rs:244-261). Distinct from the filed retraction items (todo.md:59 ground-conditional stale/hang, :75 Count witnesses, :82 replay path).
sources: logji universal rule compilation + session layer
confirmed.check_hint: Assert the same ground fact via two records, retract one id, query (expect False) and list_facts (expect a live asserting record); build a multi-root ground buffer, retract it, and probe later-root facts for survival.

adjudication.evidence:
Probe (temp integration test logji/tests/tmp_probe_retraction_finding.rs, run via `cargo test -p logji --test ... -- --test-threads=1`, deleted after) plus source reading confirms all four sub-claims, three fully:

(1) CONFIRMED (major, user-reachable): duplicate ground facts conflated. InMemoryFactStore is HashSet-backed with no multiplicity (logji/src/fact_store.rs:52-53; insert :84-91 is a no-op on duplicates; remove :106-117 deletes unconditionally). retract_fact_inner removes the fact without checking whether another active FactRecord still asserts it (logji/src/lib.rs:186-202). Reachable via REPL `:assert`/WIT assert-fact: nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:411-425 builds a single-Predicate buffer with no Exists/ForAll, so the incremental path gate (lib.rs:160-172) passes. Probe output: after asserting gerku(adam) under two records and retracting one, query=False while list_facts shows 1 active record (label rec2); kb.rebuild() then restores it to True — the incremental and rebuild paths give contradictory KB states from the same registry.

(2) CONFIRMED at the logji public-API level, reachability narrower than implied: incremental retraction collects leaves only from `record.buffer.roots.first()` (lib.rs:177-183) while process_assertion loops ALL roots (kb.rs:933). Probe: a 2-root ground buffer asserted under one ID, after retract_fact: broda(a)=False but brode(b)=True — post-retraction hallucination. Reachability caveat: Lojban-compiled buffers carry ExistsNode (event decomposition) so they take the rebuild path (lib.rs:160-167), and assert_fact_direct is single-root; the bug bites only callers of the pub `logji::KnowledgeBase::assert_fact(LogicBuffer)` API with hand-built multi-root ground buffers.

(3) CONFIRMED (major): non-atomic assertion. assert_fact_inner runs process_assertion with `?` BEFORE inserting the FactRecord (lib.rs:90-107); process_assertion asserts ground facts per-root into the store (kb.rs:1016-1018) and can fail on a later root via the stratification check (rules.rs:255-270, propagated at rules.rs:720 / kb.rs:989). Probe: buffer [root0=broda(orphan_a), root1=unstratifiable rule q:-not q] → assert returns Err("Unstratifiable negation..."), list_facts is EMPTY, yet query broda(orphan_a)=True — an orphaned, unretractable fact with no provenance record. Reachable in principle from surfaces: nibli-engine assert_text compiles a whole multi-sentence text into ONE buffer/one fact ID (nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:384-408), and NAF antecedents (supported from Lojban) can form negative cycles. Bonus defect: the error path also leaks `current_assertion_id = Some(id)` (lib.rs:93-95 have no cleanup on `?`), so rules registered later (e.g. during a rebuild) can be mis-attributed in rule_source_map.

(4) PARTIAL: rebuild_inner does swallow replay errors with `let _ = process_assertion(...)` (lib.rs:259-261) and does clear predicate_registry (lib.rs:244), and replayed signatures are re-inferred with `arg_sorts: vec![]` (rules.rs:414) with nothing restoring set_predicate_sorts declarations (lib.rs:660-674) — while entity_sorts/sort_hierarchy survive (not in the reset list, lib.rs:232-246), an inconsistency. BUT impact is weaker than stated: (a) the only Err source in process_assertion is check_stratification, which is explicitly skipped during rebuild (`if !inner.rebuilding`, rules.rs:255), so no replay error is currently reachable to be swallowed — latent fragility only; (b) the arg_sorts guard is permissive (eprintln warning only, rules.rs:421-447), so the wipe silently drops validation warnings, not reasoning verdicts.

Distinctness verified against todo.md: the filed items (Count-witness incremental leak at lib.rs:160-202/rules.rs, assert_fact_with_id missing current_assertion_id, ground-conditional retraction stale/hang) are different defects; none cover multiplicity conflation, the roots.first() asymmetry, assertion atomicity, or the registry wipe. Git status restored to the three expected modified files.
adjudication.fair_wording: Belief revision (a headline book feature, Ch 12) has registry/store consistency holes, probe-confirmed: (1) MAJOR — the HashSet fact store tracks no multiplicity and retraction doesn't consult other live records, so retracting one of two records asserting the same ground fact (reachable via REPL `:assert`/assert-fact) makes queries return False while list_facts still shows an active record asserting it; a subsequent rebuild() resurrects the fact, so the incremental and rebuild paths yield contradictory KBs (logji/src/lib.rs:186-202, fact_store.rs:84-117). (2) MAJOR — assertion is not atomic: process_assertion mutates the store per-root and a later-root failure (e.g. unstratifiable rule, reachable via multi-sentence assert_text) returns Err after earlier roots' facts are stored but before any FactRecord exists, leaving orphaned, unretractable facts that queries report True with zero registry records; the error path also leaks current_assertion_id (lib.rs:90-107, kb.rs:1016-1021, rules.rs:269). (3) MODERATE — incremental retraction processes only roots.first() while assertion processes all roots, so multi-root ground buffers leave later-root facts alive after retraction; mitigated in practice because Lojban-compiled buffers contain ExistsNode and take the rebuild path — bites only programmatic users of the public assert_fact(LogicBuffer) API (lib.rs:177-183). (4) MODERATE/MINOR — rebuild clears predicate_registry, permanently discarding user-declared per-predicate arg sorts (warnings-only guard, so diagnostics are lost, not verdicts), and `let _ = process_assertion(...)` would swallow replay errors, though none are currently reachable since stratification is skipped during rebuild (lib.rs:244,259-261; rules.rs:255,414,421). All four are distinct from the retraction issues already filed in todo.md.

confirmed.title: best_result wipe in backward chaining: a later failing rule erases pending ResourceExceeded, caching a wrong definitive False (wrong True under NAF)
confirmed.detail: 'best_result = rule_pending.and_then(|r| prefer_non_definitive(best_result, r))' at logji/src/reasoning.rs:823 (replicated at 791, 937, 968) resets best_result to None whenever a later rule for the same conclusion fails definitively, discarding an earlier rule's pending ResourceExceeded(Depth). The wrong definitive False is cached cross-depth AND halts iterative deepening before the true proof depth; via negate_result the wrong False becomes a wrong True under NAF. Reviewers disagree on reachability: one probe-confirmed it (qqq->ppp + ttt->ppp + 3-hop chain makes entailed ppp(alis) return False; control without the competing rule returns True); the other assesses it latent through the surface pipeline (per-rule SkolemFn conclusion names prevent competing unification) — adversarial verification should settle whether surface Lojban reaches it. Any fix must land in all four copy-pasted loop variants (the 4x duplication and the traced/untraced evaluator divergence flagged by several reviewers are ALREADY FILED — todo.md:52, 63, 77-78, 131, 138 — do not re-verify those).
sources: logji universal rule compilation + session layer
confirmed.check_hint: Reproduce the competing-rules probe (two rules concluding ppp, one with a chain just past the per-pass depth) and check the entailment verdict with/without the competitor; then attempt the same shape through surface Lojban to settle reachability.

adjudication.evidence: Wipe confirmed in code at all four cited sites: logji/src/reasoning.rs:823 and 791 (untraced rule loop), 936-937 and 968 (temporal-lifting loop) — `best_result = rule_pending.and_then(|r| prefer_non_definitive(best_result, r))`. When a later rule whose conclusion unifies the same fact fails definitively, rule_pending is None (reasoning.rs:808-811/953-956; combo_pending analogously zeroed at 771-774/916-919), and None.and_then(...) yields None, erasing an earlier rule's pending ResourceExceeded(Depth). reasoning.rs:974 then returns definitive False; reasoning.rs:639-643 caches it in TYPED_PRED_CACHE (cross-depth tabling); lib.rs:310 halts iterative deepening on any non-ResourceExceeded result; reasoning.rs:81-88 (negate_result) flips the wrong False to wrong True under NAF. Probe 1 (temp test in logji/src/tests.rs using the crate's own FOL-buffer helpers, removed after run): facts c0(alis) + rules c0→c1→c2→c3→qqq→ppp; query ppp(alis) = True (control); after adding competitor rule ttt→ppp registered later, same entailed query = False, and NAF query ¬ppp(alis) = True (output: 'PROBE control: True / PROBE with competitor: False / PROBE NAF na-ppp(alis): True'). Reachability dispute settled in favor of LATENT through surface Lojban: Probe 2 (temp nibli-engine integration test, removed after run) — 'la .alis. cu gerku' + 'ro lo gerku cu danlu' + 'ro lo danlu cu jmive' + 'ro lo jmive cu prenu' + competitor 'ro lo mlatu cu prenu' — query 'la .alis. cu prenu' returns True with and without the competitor; 'la .alis. na prenu' correctly False. Structural reason: the wipe executes only after successful unification (unify failure hits `continue` at reasoning.rs:694-696 leaving best_result untouched); smuni-compiled universal rules put a rule-unique SkolemFn base name in every conclusion atom (per-KB monotonic counter kb.rs:603-607) and unify_terms requires exact SkolemFn base-name equality (kb.rs:310-316); ground ganai conditionals have assertion-unique skolem-constant conclusions; retraction rebuild resets the skolem counter only together with clearing universal_rules (lib.rs:231-246), preserving the invariant; fact-injection surfaces (assert-fact WIT, REPL :assert, engine assert_fact_direct) inject facts, not rules. So no shipped runtime surface (gasnu REPL, nibli-server/UI, session WIT) can create competing-unification rules; the bug is live only for direct logji crate embedders or the legacy logji-component WIT world fed hand-built (non-event-decomposed) FOL buffers — exactly the shape logji's own test helpers produce.
adjudication.fair_wording: Confirmed unsoundness in logji's core evaluator, latent through the shipped Lojban pipeline. In try_backward_chain_typed, a later rule that unifies the queried fact and fails definitively wipes an earlier rule's pending ResourceExceeded via `rule_pending.and_then(|r| prefer_non_definitive(best_result, r))` (logji/src/reasoning.rs:791, 823, 937, 968): the entailed query then returns a definitive False that is cached cross-depth (reasoning.rs:639-643) and halts iterative deepening before the true proof depth (lib.rs:310), and negation-as-failure converts it to a wrong True (reasoning.rs:83). Probe-confirmed through logji's public FOL contract (two rules concluding ppp, one fed by a 5-hop chain: entailed ppp(alis) returns False, ¬ppp(alis) returns True; control without the competitor returns True). Adversarial probing settles the reachability dispute: surface Lojban cannot trigger it, because every smuni-compiled conclusion atom carries a rule-unique SkolemFn base name that unify_facts matches exactly (kb.rs:310-316, counter at kb.rs:604), ground conditionals have assertion-unique conclusions, rebuild resets counter and rules together, and fact-injection APIs cannot register rules — a surface probe with competing rules concluding the same relation answers correctly. The defect is real wrong-answer unsoundness guarded only by an incidental, unenforced representation invariant; any embedder feeding plain FOL (the documented logji interface, as logji's own tests do) gets wrong False/True today. Fix: replace the and_then with a merge that never discards best_result when rule_pending is None (e.g. `if let Some(r) = rule_pending { best_result = prefer_non_definitive(best_result, r) }`), applied to all four loop variants, plus a regression test mirroring the competing-rules probe.

confirmed.title: Persistence/belief-revision durability broken at every layer: WasiFactStore dead via its trait, gasnu replay fact-ID drift, engine retraction never persisted
confirmed.detail: (1) WasiFactStore is unusable through the FactStore trait the KB consumes: lookup_predicate/contains consult only the LRU cache and lazy loading requires the inherent load_predicate(&mut self) that the trait lacks and the KB never calls — a reopened store silently presents an EMPTY KB; remove leaves pred_index offsets so removed facts resurrect after LRU eviction; in-file tests call load_predicate directly, masking the hole; the book stakes Ch12 on this store (logji/src/wasi_fact_store.rs:194-242). (2) gasnu startup replay re-asserts via call_assert_text and discards new engine IDs while redb keeps the originals: :retract tombstones the WRONG record, retracted facts resurrect on restart, and a colliding new ID makes insert_fact silently OVERWRITE a live persisted fact — assert_fact_with_id exists for exactly this but is never exposed/used (gasnu/src/main.rs:740-746, 1037). (3) NibliEngine::retract_fact never calls NibliStore::retract_fact, so retraction is undone on reopen; the one integration test sidesteps the engine API by retracting at the store; replay_from_store drops a must_use Result (unused_must_use warning, contradicting the compiles-cleanly claim) (nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:489, 313). Fixture cleanup misses <name>.typed.redb — test isolation is accidental (integration.rs:36-38).
confirmed.check_hint: Reopen a WasiFactStore and query through the FactStore trait only (expect empty); on a persistent gasnu KB, retract mid-history, restart, and watch resurrection/overwrite; call NibliEngine::retract_fact on a persistent engine, reopen, and query the fact.

adjudication.evidence:
(1) WasiFactStore unusable through the FactStore trait — CONFIRMED, with one correction. logji/src/wasi_fact_store.rs:194-200: `lookup_predicate` returns `self.cache.get(relation)`; the comment "The KB calls ensure_loaded() before queries" is false — `ensure_loaded` exists nowhere in the repo (grep: 0 hits). The lazy loader `load_predicate` (line 93) is module-private and absent from the trait (logji/src/fact_store.rs:16-45), so no consumer can ever trigger a disk load: a reopened store reports `len()` from the index (line 74-88) but `lookup_predicate`=None, `contains`=false, `all_facts()`=empty. The store's own test documents the hole (line 341 asserts `lookup_predicate("gerku").is_none()` after reopen, then calls private `load_predicate` at 344/352 to proceed); ran them: 5/5 pass with `--features wasi-store`. `remove` (lines 229-242) leaves `pred_index` offsets and Drop flushes the stale index (line 280-284), so removed facts persist in the on-disk index. Correction: the `wasi-store` feature is enabled by NO workspace crate (grep all Cargo.tomls) — this is dead code never compiled into any binary; and the book reference is Ch7, not Ch12: book/P3_C07_The_Indexed_Fact_Store_and_Backward_Chaining.md:724 claims "When backward chaining requests facts for a predicate not in cache, the backend loads them from disk on demand" — impossible as written. Ch12 covers only in-memory belief revision and makes no WASI-store claim.
(2) gasnu replay ID drift — CONFIRMED by code reading (deterministic ID arithmetic; live REPL probe impractical: reedline rejects piped stdin). gasnu/src/main.rs:740-746 and 766-772: replay discards the fresh session's returned fact-id (`Ok(Ok(_)) => replayed += 1`); session IDs restart at 0 (logji/src/kb.rs:550,597-600), while redb keeps original IDs. Any tombstone or replay error compacts the sequence: store {0 active, 1 tombstoned, 2 active} replays as engine IDs {0,1}; `:retract 1` (main.rs:1035-1039) retracts engine fact 1 (= store record 2) but calls `s.retract_fact(1)` — a silent no-op on the already-tombstoned record 1, so record 2 resurrects on next restart. Next new assertion gets engine ID 2; persist_text (main.rs:573-580) → NibliStore::insert_fact (nibli-store/src/lib.rs:311-336) `table.insert(id, ...)` silently OVERWRITES live record 2 (redb insert-replaces). Correction: `assert_fact_with_id` (logji/src/lib.rs:111) IS used — by nibli-engine replay (lib.rs:313) — but it is not in the WIT session/knowledge-base interface (wit/world.wit:453,499-515), so gasnu cannot reach it across the WASM boundary.
(3) Engine retraction not durable — CONFIRMED BY PROBE. nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:489-491 calls only `kb.retract_fact`, never `NibliStore::retract_fact`. Probe (temp test, since deleted): open persistent engine, assert "la .adam. cu gerku", `engine.retract_fact(id)` → query FALSE and empty registry; reopen → query TRUE, list_facts=1. Retracted fact resurrects. The one retraction-persistence integration test (nibli-engine/tests/integration.rs:383-413) retracts via NibliStore directly, bypassing the engine API. The dropped must_use Result is real: cargo emitted `warning: unused 'Result' that must be used --> nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:313` (so replay errors are silently swallowed), contradicting CLAUDE.md's "All crates compile cleanly" (plus a second warning: unused import nibli-store/src/typed_store.rs:15). New related defects found: RedbFactStore::insert (typed_store.rs:140-169) appends a new row unconditionally and the assertion path never checks contains (logji/src/rules.rs:463), so every reopen's replay duplicates every fact's redb row — unbounded typed.redb growth per restart; and RedbFactStore::remove (typed_store.rs:216-225) is cache-only ("disk cleanup deferred to compaction"), so even the typed layer's incremental retraction path is non-durable.
(4) Fixture cleanup — CONFIRMED BY PROBE. integration.rs:36-38 cleanup removes only `<name>.redb`; NibliEngine::open creates `<name>.typed.redb` (lib.rs:286). Probe confirmed the typed file survives cleanup and is eagerly reloaded next run (typed_store.rs:56-84). Tests still pass only because orphaned typed facts lack KB-side entity/domain registrations, so event-decomposed existential queries can't see them — isolation is accidental.
adjudication.fair_wording: MAJOR. Belief-revision durability is broken at every persistence layer, and the only tests covering it are shaped around the holes. (a) NibliEngine::retract_fact never propagates to the replay store: probe-verified that a retracted fact resurrects on reopen (nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:489 vs 313); the lone integration test (integration.rs:383) sidesteps the engine API by retracting at the store, and the dropped must_use Result at lib.rs:313 both swallows replay errors and breaks the "compiles cleanly" claim. (b) gasnu's restart replay discards re-assigned fact IDs (gasnu/src/main.rs:740-746) while redb keeps the originals, so after any tombstone or replay error, :retract tombstones the wrong record (resurrection) and a colliding new ID silently overwrites a live persisted fact via NibliStore::insert_fact's insert-replace semantics (nibli-store/src/lib.rs:332); the fix primitive (assert_fact_with_id) exists in logji and is used by nibli-engine but is not exposed through WIT for gasnu. (c) WasiFactStore cannot function through the FactStore trait — its lazy loader is private and referenced only by a comment naming a nonexistent ensure_loaded(); a reopened store is silently empty and remove() leaves resurrectable index offsets — though it is feature-gated dead code no crate enables, making this false book documentation (Ch7 line 724, not Ch12 as reported) rather than a live data path. (d) Probe-verified that integration-test cleanup leaves stale .typed.redb files that reload on the next run; isolation holds only by accident. Additional new defects: every reopen's replay appends duplicate rows to the typed redb store (unbounded growth), and RedbFactStore::remove is cache-only, so the typed layer's incremental retraction is also non-durable.

confirmed.title: lasna go'i snapshot is drained by every query path: the next go'i call traps the entire WASM session
confirmed.detail: graft_snapshot drains the stored snapshot's vecs via Vec::append, and only a successful assert_text refreshes last_relation; query_text, query_find_text, query_text_with_proof, compile_debug, and the assert warnings-abort path all graft-and-drain WITHOUT refreshing, so the NEXT go'i-containing call grafts empty vecs and resolve_go_i returns an out-of-bounds selbri index -> smuni indexing panic -> WASM trap (lasna/src/lib.rs:438-456, 580-584, 682-732; reproduced; REPL repro: 'mi klama' / '? go'i' / '? go'i'). The 1090-line Session WIT surface has zero tests beyond 13 snapshot-helper units; a single assert/query/query lifecycle test would have caught this. Not filed in todo.md.
confirmed.check_hint: In the gasnu REPL: 'mi klama', then '? go'i' twice — second call should trap. Inspect graft_snapshot's Vec::append drain and which call sites refresh last_relation.

Under wasm32 panic=abort this is a guest trap; gasnu's "?" REPL path is call_query_text_with_proof (gasnu/src/main.rs:1275). Two corrections of detail, neither changing the outcome: (a) the panic fires in lasna's own snapshot re-extraction, before smuni (smuni would equally panic — unchecked indexing at smuni/src/semantic/selbri.rs:46); (b) drain occurs only on go'i-CONTAINING calls (has_go_i guard, lib.rs:576-584), not on every query — the headline "every query path" is overbroad, but the repro and all listed call sites are exact.
