(5) Coverage: exactly 13 #[test] fns in lasna (lib.rs:848-1087), all snapshot/resolve helpers, zero Session assert/query lifecycle tests; no go'i tests in nibli-engine; grep confirms no go'i entry in todo.md.
adjudication.fair_wording: lasna's go'i snapshot is single-use by construction: graft_snapshot (lasna/src/lib.rs:452-454) drains the stored snapshot's vecs in place via Vec::append, and only a successful assert_text refreshes last_relation (line 674). All other graft sites — query_text, query_find_text, query_text_with_proof, compile_debug, and assert_text's warnings-abort path — leave the drained husk behind, so the next go'i-containing call grafts empty vecs and produces a dangling selbri index, panicking (reproduced: "index out of bounds" at lasna/src/lib.rs:275 during snapshot re-extraction; smuni's unchecked indexing at smuni/src/semantic/selbri.rs:46 would panic equally). In the WASM component a panic is a trap, killing the session and its entire KB from two well-formed REPL inputs ("mi klama", then "? go'i" twice) — a direct violation of the typed nibli-error contract the book claims for Part IV. Severity: major. Additionally, go'i inside abstractions or relative-clause bodies is never resolved (resolve_sentence_go_i only checks the top-level relation), so "mi nelci lo nu go'i" silently asserts a literal predicate named go'i — a fabricated fact in a zero-hallucination engine (moderate). The lasna Session surface has only 13 snapshot-helper unit tests and no assert/query lifecycle test; the bug is not filed in todo.md. Minor caveat on the original wording: the drain happens only on go'i-containing calls, not literally every query.

confirmed.title: Hardening claims fail on the canonical runtimes: server reasoning unbounded and uncancellable; gasnu sessions poisoned after traps
confirmed.detail: nibli-server — the canonical Transparency Triad API handling untrusted HTTP — runs NibliEngine::new() natively with NO fuel and NO memory limit (only logji's depth-10 cap), so the Part IV 'fuel and memory limits bound adversarial input' claim is false on the very path that needs it (nibli-server/src/main.rs:707-738). The 30s timeout only abandons the response: spawn_blocking work runs to completion, so each pathological query (logji has filed non-terminating queries) returns 408 but permanently pins a blocking-pool thread at 100% CPU; repeated requests exhaust the 512-thread pool (main.rs:1573-1586, 485-493). Rate limiting is one GLOBAL unkeyed bucket (any client starves all others; RPS=0 panics at startup), and Schema::build sets no GraphQL depth/complexity limit with introspection always on (main.rs:98, 1687-1701). On gasnu: after a fuel/memory trap the REPL keeps calling the same session — traps don't unwind the guest, logji's RefCell borrow flag stays set in linear memory and the KB is left half-mutated (unsound derivations possible); no re-instantiation path exists and the '[Limit]' message implies retry is safe; trap classification is by message substring instead of wasmtime::Trap downcast (gasnu/src/main.rs:612-623). Not filed in todo.md.
sources: python compute backend + Transparency Triad server/UI
confirmed.check_hint: Fire a known non-terminating query at queryWithKb; confirm 408 plus a pinned blocking thread, then repeat to exhaust the pool. On gasnu, set tiny NIBLI_FUEL, trip it mid-assert, and issue the next command — observe in-guest RefCell panic or inconsistent KB.

adjudication.evidence:
Every sub-claim verified against code; the central gasnu claim verified by an empirical wasmtime probe (added to gasnu tests, run, then reverted — working tree restored).

SERVER (nibli-server, the canonical untrusted-HTTP Transparency Triad API):
1. NO fuel/NO memory limit on reasoning. NibliEngine wraps logji::KnowledgeBase NATIVELY (nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:243 `kb: logji::KnowledgeBase`, :272 `KnowledgeBase::new()`); there is no wasmtime/StoreLimits/consume_fuel anywhere in nibli-engine. The only bound is logji's internal max_chain_depth. query_with_kb (nibli-server/src/main.rs:707-738) runs this engine on attacker-supplied `kb`+`query` strings. So the Part IV "fuel and memory limits bound adversarial input" claim does NOT hold on this runtime — it holds only inside gasnu's WASM host.
2. Timeout is cosmetic / uncancellable. Queries run via run_blocking -> task::spawn_blocking (main.rs:485-493). request_timeout_middleware (main.rs:1573-1586) only wraps `next.run()` in tokio::time::timeout and returns 408 on elapse; spawn_blocking work is NOT cancellable and runs to completion. No abort() exists on this path (the abort() calls at main.rs:2046/2282/etc. are all gossip-transport tasks, out of scope). #[tokio::main] (main.rs:1646) with no max_blocking_threads override => default 512-thread blocking pool. Combined with the FILED non-terminating query (todo.md:59, ">200s, killed by timeout"), each 408'd pathological request permanently pins a blocking thread; repeats exhaust the pool.
3. Global unkeyed rate limiter: SharedRateLimiter = RateLimiter<NotKeyed,...> (main.rs:98), one Quota::per_second bucket (main.rs:1698-1701). One client starves all others.
4. RATE_LIMIT_RPS=0 panics at startup: parsed via .parse().ok().unwrap_or(50) (main.rs:164) so "0" => 0u32, then NonZeroU32::new(config.rate_limit_rps).expect("rate_limit_rps must be > 0") (main.rs:1699) panics. Config footgun validated only by panic.
5. GraphQL unbounded: Schema::build(QueryRoot, MutationRoot, EmptySubscription)...finish() (main.rs:1687-1695) sets no .limit_depth()/.limit_complexity()/.disable_introspection(); introspection always on.

GASNU (empirical probe — added test, ran under Nix, then reverted; gasnu/src/main.rs git-clean afterward):
- Architecture: one Store + one session + one session_handle created ONCE before the REPL loop (gasnu/src/main.rs:687-700) and reused for every command.
- Probe result (rustc 1.94.0, lasna.wasm): baseline assert OK; a low-fuel assert TRAPPED mid-parse; then EVERY subsequent call on the same store/session returned "wasm trap: cannot enter component instance" (post-trap assert AND post-trap query both Err). The component-model reentrancy guard permanently bricks the instance after any trap — WORSE than the reported "RefCell poisoned / KB half-mutated": the whole session is dead. The RefCell guard's Drop indeed never runs (borrow held across reasoning at logji/src/lib.rs:290,323,378 and across process_assertion at :91-94), but the observable failure is the instance lock.
- REPL has NO re-instantiation path; on a host Err it just prints and loops (gasnu/src/main.rs:1281,1291) — so after one trap, assert/query/:reset/:facts all fail.
- format_host_error (gasnu/src/main.rs:612-623) MIS-classified the real fuel trap: it returned "[Host Error] <wasm backtrace>" NOT "[Limit] ...", because e.to_string() yields only the top context line "error while executing at wasm backtrace:" (no "fuel"/"memory" substring at that level). So the substring classification is unreliable and the [Limit] hint often won't appear.

None of the server/gasnu hardening gaps are filed in todo.md; only the underlying non-terminating query (todo.md:59) is filed.
adjudication.fair_wording: The Part IV hardening claims fail on both canonical runtimes. nibli-server runs reasoning via a NATIVE NibliEngine (logji::KnowledgeBase, nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:243,272) with NO fuel and NO memory limit — only logji's max_chain_depth — so "fuel/memory limits bound adversarial input" is false on query_with_kb (nibli-server/src/main.rs:707-738), the untrusted-HTTP path; fuel/memory bounding exists only in gasnu's WASM host. The 30s timeout (main.rs:1573-1586) is cosmetic: queries run in task::spawn_blocking (main.rs:485-493), which is uncancellable, so a 408'd pathological query (the filed non-terminating case, todo.md:59) runs to completion and pins one of the default 512 blocking-pool threads; repeats exhaust the pool. Rate limiting is a single global unkeyed bucket (RateLimiter<NotKeyed>, main.rs:98) — one client starves all — and RATE_LIMIT_RPS=0 panics at startup (NonZeroU32::new(0).expect, main.rs:1699). The GraphQL schema sets no depth/complexity limit and leaves introspection on (Schema::build...finish(), main.rs:1687-1695). On gasnu, a probe confirmed the soundness/availability claim and showed it is actually worse than reported: the REPL creates one Store/session once (main.rs:687-700) and reuses it; after any fuel/memory trap, every subsequent call returns "wasm trap: cannot enter component instance" — the whole component instance is permanently bricked, not merely RefCell-poisoned, and there is no re-instantiation path (the loop just prints the error, main.rs:1281/1291). format_host_error (main.rs:612-623) also mis-classifies the fuel trap as "[Host Error]" rather than "[Limit]" because the substring match runs on e.to_string()'s top line, which lacks "fuel"/"memory". None of these gaps are filed in todo.md.

confirmed.title: Verification gap: chapter-narrated GDPR queries do not reproduce against the shipped corpus, the WASM runtime is never tested, filed unsoundness is unpinned
confirmed.detail: The integration banner claims gdpr.lojban is 'the single source of truth', but only gdpr_file_loads_clean touches it (assert-only, zero queries) — all six gdpr_* reasoning tests rebuild 3-5 line inline KBs. Probed against the actually-loaded corpus: the lawful-basis query takes 3.3s and Ch20's verbatim breach-notification query did not return within ~230s — readers following Ch20's ':load gdpr.lojban' instructions hit a hang the suite cannot see (integration.rs:466-629). DDI corpus modeling: 'la .varfarin. cu kajde' stays True after retracting the patient's ENTIRE regimen (alert independent of exposure — pilno facts never enter the mechanism chain), and the belief-revision test retracts the pharmacological constant rather than the prescription (drug-interactions.lojban:59-80). Test architecture: 'just ci' never builds the lasna WASM component; fuel/memory/sandbox claims are 'tested' by compile-only smoke tests (gasnu/src/main.rs:1517-1595); filed unsoundness classes (todo.md:58-59, 82) have ZERO pinning/#[ignore] tests so a rule that never fires is invisible; criterion benches query structurally unmatchable shapes (timing only failure paths, engine_bench.rs:43-163); fuzz targets have no seed corpus/dictionary and check no properties; the count quantifier lacks an N+1 overcount control. todo.md treats both corpora as 'fixed' — the non-reproducibility and these test holes are unfiled.
confirmed.check_hint: ':load gdpr.lojban' then run Ch20's breach-notification query under timeout (expect hang vs the chapter's captured output); grep the gdpr_* tests for corpus usage; retract the DDI patient's pilno facts and re-query kajde (expect it stays True); confirm 'just ci' never builds/runs the lasna component.

adjudication.evidence:
CENTRAL CLAIM — chapter queries do not reproduce against the shipped corpus — CONFIRMED, and worse than reported. Ch20 (book/P5_C20...md:109) tells readers to `:load gdpr.lojban` then run `? la .adam. cu se curmi → RESULT: True` "captured verbatim from the engine" (Ch20:162-167). Probe: full gdpr.lojban loads in 4.99ms, but querying `la .adam. cu se curmi` (lawful basis) against the loaded corpus did NOT return within 240s (debug build; finding said 3.3s — that was likely release). The breach-notification query `la .akmes. cu se bilga lo nu notci` (Ch20:339) and the clean control both hung past 60s. So the chapter's "verbatim" outputs cannot have come from the full corpus. The integration banner (nibli-engine/tests/integration.rs:466-467) asserts "The corpus file (gdpr.lojban) is the single source of truth" — false: only gdpr_file_loads_clean (integration.rs:471-484) touches the full corpus and it is assert-only (zero queries); all six gdpr_* reasoning tests (integration.rs:536-629) query 3-5 line inline KBs that avoid the many-universal-rule blowup the full corpus triggers.

WASM RUNTIME NEVER TESTED — CONFIRMED. `just ci` (Justfile:160) runs fmt-check/clippy/test/test-engine/test-store/test-server/gossip — no `build-wasm`, no test that loads the lasna WASM component. Integration tests are native (integration.rs:4-5: "No WASM, no HTTP"). gasnu fuel/memory/sandbox "tests" (gasnu/src/main.rs:1516-1596) are compile-only: test_make_host_has_limits comment says "If it compiles and runs, the limits field is present" (1520); test_store_limiter_integration ends "Store is set up with both fuel and memory limits — if no panic, success" (1595) and never runs a component to exhaustion; the four test_format_host_error_* tests only assert string formatting. No behavioral fuel-exhaustion or memory-cap test exists — the Part IV "production engineering" claim is untested.

FILED UNSOUNDNESS UNPINNED — CONFIRMED. grep found ZERO `#[ignore]` tests across nibli-engine/logji/gasnu/smuni/gerna. todo.md:58 (binary-antecedent universal rule never fires), :59 (retract+rebuild stale/hang >200s), :82 — each item's own "Fix" note says "add a regression test," i.e. none exists. A rule that never fires is invisible to the suite.

DDI MODELING DEFECTS — CONFIRMED by inspection + probe. In drug-interactions.lojban, pilno (patient regimen, lines 59-60) appears in NO rule antecedent (rules at 71-72, 79-80, 85). Probe: built the warfarin mechanism + Adam's regimen, queried `la .varfarin. cu kajde` → True; retracted BOTH pilno facts (regimen empty) → still True. The alert is a property of the drug, independent of co-prescription, though the corpus header (line 4) frames the question as "should this co-prescription raise a safety alert?". The belief-revision test ddi_belief_revision_discontinue_inhibitor (integration.rs:843) retracts `la .flukonazol. cu fanta la .siptucin.` ("fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9") — a permanent pharmacological constant — to simulate "the interacting drug is discontinued" (test comment 800-804); clinically inverted, since discontinuing a drug stops exposure (pilno), it does not make the drug cease to be an enzyme inhibitor.

UNFILED — CONFIRMED. todo.md:3 and :120 both state "Both case-study corpora are now fixed." No todo item files the full-corpus GDPR query hang (todo.md:59 files a retract+rebuild hang — a different path; a plain query with no retraction also hangs).

SECONDARY (minor). engine_bench.rs:43-55 bench_query_latency populates gerku(ent0..entN) but queries `la .adam. gerku` (adam never asserted) → measures the no-match/FALSE path. fuzz_query.rs: no fuzz/corpus dir, no dictionary, panic-only (asserts no property such as assert→query roundtrip or determinism). No N+1 overcount test for the count quantifier (todo.md:46 files the related dedup-overcount concern).
adjudication.fair_wording: CONFIRMED. The headline — chapter-narrated GDPR queries do not reproduce against the shipped corpus, the WASM runtime/fuel/memory/sandbox enforcement is never behaviorally tested, and filed unsoundness has zero pinning tests — is verified, with the GDPR hang being more severe than reported (even the first lawful-basis query exceeds 240s against the loaded corpus in a debug build; the finding's 3.3s was likely a release/partial measurement). Two sub-claims are slightly overbroad and should be narrowed: (1) "benches query structurally unmatchable shapes / timing only failure paths" is true specifically for bench_query_latency (engine_bench.rs:43-55); bench_rule_chain and bench_witness_extraction do match real data, so the bench suite is not uniformly measuring failure paths. (2) The exact lawful-basis timing is build-mode dependent. The DDI regimen-independence and belief-revision-retracts-a-pharmacological-constant defects are confirmed by both static inspection (pilno never enters any rule antecedent) and a dynamic probe (alert stays True after the full regimen is retracted). Severity: the non-reproducibility against the shipped corpus combined with the "single source of truth"/"captured verbatim" claims is a broken-contract defect for a book that sells reproducibility; the untested WASM/fuel/memory layer and unpinned known-unsoundness are real test-debt against the production-aspiring framing.

partial.title: Quantifier-closure scoping defects: da/de/di existential wrapped OUTSIDE the universal, ma closed in the wrong bridi, free variables escape
partial.detail: The da/de/di closure at smuni/src/semantic/compile.rs:244-252 runs AFTER the description-quantifier loop, so 'ro lo gerku cu citka da' compiles to Exists(da).ForAll(x) instead of Lojban's left-to-right ForAll(x).Exists(da) — probe-confirmed: da Skolemizes to one shared constant, asserting the strictly stronger 'one thing all dogs eat'. Separately, ma_vars is compiler-level state drained at the end of EVERY compile_bridi (compile.rs:254), so 'ma nelci lo gerku poi barda' closes ma's existential inside the rel-clause body while the main-body occurrence stays free — breaks ?? witness extraction whenever ma combines with poi (verified). And the closure scan covers only the main bridi arg list: da via a BAI modal ('mi klama ri'a da') or be/bei bound arg flows into logji as a free variable. All three are zero-hallucination violations on the assertion path. Not filed in todo.md.
partial.check_hint: Compile 'ro lo gerku cu citka da' and inspect Skolem dependence (shared constant vs forall-dependent SkolemFn); compile 'ma nelci lo gerku poi barda' and check which scope binds _v0; compile 'mi klama ri'a da' and look for an unclosed Var.

adjudication.evidence:
All three defects EXIST and were probe-verified, but two of the finding's specific behavioral characterizations are wrong; one is wrong in a direction that makes the bug WORSE than reported.

(1) da/de/di closed outside the universal — CONFIRMED, but consequence mischaracterized. smuni/src/semantic/compile.rs:157-242 wraps ForAll for `ro lo` quantifiers, then :244-252 wraps Exists(da) OUTSIDE it. Probe (nibli-engine compile_debug): `ro lo gerku cu citka da` → `(Exists "da" (ForAll "_v0" ...))` — inverted vs Lojban left-to-right ∀x.∃y. Skolemization (logji/src/rules.rs:35-47, root-first) printed `da ↦ sk_1` (plain constant, NOT ∀-dependent; control `ro lo gerku cu citka lo plise` printed `_v1 ↦ sk_2(∀-dependent)`). HOWEVER the finding's claim that the engine "asserts the strictly stronger 'one thing all dogs eat'" is operationally false — it's worse: because the root is Exists, the `is_forall` dispatch (logji/src/kb.rs:973) takes the GROUND path; collect_ground_facts (rules.rs:935-972) and register_ground_material_conditional (kb.rs:884-927) both dead-end at the ForAllNode (`_` fallthrough), so NO rule is registered and NO facts stored. Probe: no `[Rule] Compiled` line; assert returns Ok(1) as if successful; query `la .alis. cu citka da` → False (control with `lo plise` → True). Silent total assertion loss with success reported = major zero-hallucination violation, just not the one described.

(2) ma_vars drained in the wrong bridi — IR defect CONFIRMED, claimed ?? breakage REFUTED. ma_vars is compiler-level state (smuni/src/semantic.rs:81) pushed at helpers.rs:104 and drained at the end of EVERY compile_bridi (compile.rs:254-256); the Restricted arm re-enters compile_bridi via compile_sentence (helpers.rs:219-220), stealing pending ma vars when `ma` precedes a `poi` clause. Probe form for `ma nelci lo gerku poi barda`: `(Exists "_v1" (And (Exists "_v0" <barda body — _v0 absent>) (... nelci_x1(_ev1, Var "_v0") ...)))` — binder misscoped into the restrictor, main occurrence free w.r.t. it. BUT query_find_text returned the CORRECT witness (alis) in the positive case AND `[]` in the negative-discrimination case (rex gerku but not barda): logji's witness search binds by variable name regardless of binder nesting, and the misscoped ∃ over a body not containing _v0 is semantically vacuous, so the finding's "breaks ?? witness extraction whenever ma combines with poi (verified)" did not reproduce.

(3) da via BAI modal or be/bei escapes as a free variable — CONFIRMED exactly, plus fact-store corruption. The closure scan iterates only the main relation's `args` (compile.rs:245); modal terms live in modal_entries (compile.rs:100-104, 124-155) and be/bei args resolve inside Selbri::WithArgs (smuni/src/semantic/selbri.rs:117-134) — neither reaches the scan. Probe: `mi klama ri'a da` → `(Pred "rinka" (Cons (Var "da") ...))` with no Exists("da"); `lo bersa be da cu klama` → `bersa_x2(_ev1, Var "da")`, no Exists; both ASSERT successfully. logji's build_ground_term (rules.rs:878-881) maps the unsubstituted variable to GroundTerm::PatternVar("da"), which is stored as a "ground" fact — a non-ground pattern variable in the typed fact store (unify_terms kb.rs:296-304 treats template-side PatternVar as a wildcard). A surface-query wildcard exploit did not reproduce (`la .bob. cu rinka mi` → False; modal facts are flat while queries event-decompose), but the existential force of `da` is silently lost and the store holds a non-ground fact.

"Not filed in todo.md": verified — the closest entries (prenex zo'u at line 13, duplicated quantifier-closure block at line 32, fi'o arity-1 at line 38) do not cover any of these three. Probes run via a temp test file in nibli-engine/tests/ (6+2 tests, all output captured), deleted afterward; git status restored.
adjudication.fair_wording: Quantifier-closure scoping defects in smuni are real, unfiled, and on the assertion path, but two of the three reported consequences need correction. (1) MAJOR: `da` after a `ro lo` description compiles to Exists-outside-ForAll (smuni/src/semantic/compile.rs:244-252 runs after the quantifier loop at :157-242), inverting Lojban left-to-right scope; the operational effect is not the reported "asserts the stronger ∃∀ statement" but silent total assertion loss — logji's is_forall dispatch (kb.rs:973) sees Exists at root, takes the ground path, dead-ends at the inner ForAllNode, registers no rule and stores no facts, yet returns a fact id as if asserted (probe: `ro lo gerku cu citka da` + `la .alis. gerku` → query False; control with `lo plise` → True, with `[Rule] Compiled` and a ∀-dependent Skolem). (2) MODERATE: `ma_vars` is compiler-global and drained at the end of every compile_bridi (compile.rs:254), so a rel-clause body compiled mid-bridi (helpers.rs:219-220) steals a pending `ma` var — probe confirms the malformed IR (binder inside the poi restrictor, main occurrence free) — but the claimed ?? witness-extraction breakage did NOT reproduce: find queries returned correct witnesses in both positive and negative-discrimination probes, because logji binds variables by name regardless of binder nesting. Latent IR-soundness debt, not demonstrated user breakage. (3) MAJOR: `da` bound via a BAI modal (`mi klama ri'a da`) or a be/bei arg (`lo bersa be da cu klama`) is never wrapped in Exists (scan at compile.rs:245 covers only main args; modal entries and selbri.rs:117-134 WithArgs args are missed) and is asserted into the typed fact store as GroundTerm::PatternVar (rules.rs:878-881) — a non-ground "ground fact"; the existential meaning is silently lost, though no surface-query wildcard over-match was demonstrable. None of the three is filed in todo.md.

partial.title: du-equivalence fallback recurses unboundedly: stack-overflow crash (SIGABRT), process-kill DoS on the native server
partial.detail: The du-equivalence fallback recurses check_predicate_in_kb_typed(variant) with no cycle guard: try_backward_chain_typed removes its visited entry before returning, and the variant loop neither inserts into visited nor increments depth (logji/src/reasoning.rs:594-621). Probe-confirmed: assert du(alis,bob), query unprovable mlatu(bob) => unbounded P(a)->P(b)->P(a) recursion, stack overflow, SIGABRT. Reachable via the documented ':assert du' REPL command and the assert-fact WIT method; on native nibli-server (no fuel limit) this is a process-crash DoS. The traced fallback (reasoning.rs:1110-1159) shares the root cause, and the same region performs O(n^2) duplicate cartesian expansion over equivalence classes per failed predicate check (Performance audit). Not filed in todo.md (the filed du item is about du being unparseable from surface Lojban, not this crash).
partial.check_hint: ':assert du alis bob' then query an unprovable predicate about bob; observe the stack overflow. Confirm reasoning.rs:594-621 never inserts into visited nor bumps depth in the variant loop.

adjudication.evidence: CRASH CONFIRMED by probe. Added two temp tests to logji/src/tests.rs (now reverted; git clean): assert du(alis,bob); query unprovable mlatu(bob). Both the untraced path (query_entailment_inner) and traced path (query_entailment_with_proof_inner) abort: "thread ... has overflowed its stack / fatal runtime error: stack overflow, aborting ... signal: 6, SIGABRT". Root cause exactly as reported: logji/src/reasoning.rs:594-621 — the equivalence-variant fallback recurses check_predicate_in_kb_typed(&variant, inner, depth, visited) at line 613 with the SAME depth (no depth+1) and a visited set that no longer contains the original fact, because try_backward_chain_typed inserts fact into visited (line 670) but removes it at every return (lines 820, 965, 973). So P(a)->P(b)->P(a) recurses unbounded; the max_chain_depth guard never trips because depth never advances. Traced mirror confirmed at reasoning.rs:1110-1159, which at line 1129 calls check_predicate_in_kb_typed(&variant, &*inner, depth, &mut HashSet::new()) — a fresh empty visited per call — feeding the same untraced cycle. NOT filed in todo.md (the filed du items are parseability at todo.md:14 and provenance labeling at todo.md:76). DoS SCOPE NARROWER than reported: nibli-server GraphQL exposes only assert_text(Lojban)+query_text (nibli-server/src/main.rs:649,670), NO direct-fact injection; and du is not parseable from surface Lojban (no du handling found in gerna/smuni; filed limitation todo.md:14 + CLAUDE.md). So the native server endpoint cannot reach du. The surfaces that CAN inject du — gasnu REPL :assert and lasna assert-fact WIT method — run logji inside the WASM component, where a guest stack overflow is a recoverable Wasmtime trap, not a host kill. The native (no-WASM) host crash is reachable only via nibli-engine::assert_fact_direct (nibli-engine/src/lib.rs:411), an internal embedding API / its own integration tests, which CLAUDE.md classifies as not a user-facing runtime surface.
adjudication.fair_wording: PARTIAL. The core technical claim is correct and probe-confirmed: the du-equivalence variant fallback in check_predicate_in_kb_typed (logji/src/reasoning.rs:594-621) recurses with no cycle guard and no depth increment — try_backward_chain_typed removes its own visited entry before returning, and the variant loop passes the same depth and a cleaned visited — so asserting du(alis,bob) then querying an unprovable predicate about bob (e.g. mlatu(bob)) recurses unboundedly and crashes with a stack overflow / SIGABRT. Both the untraced and traced query paths crash; the traced fallback (reasoning.rs:1110-1159) shares the root cause and additionally passes a fresh HashSet::new() each call. This is a genuine, previously-unfiled non-terminating-recursion / soundness-and-termination defect in the reasoning core, in scope and held to the production aspiration. However, the headline 'process-kill DoS on the native nibli-server' is overstated. The native nibli-server's GraphQL surface only accepts Lojban text (assert_text) plus queries, and du is not parseable from surface Lojban (a documented limitation), so a client cannot inject a du fact through the server to trigger the crash. The user-facing runtimes that CAN inject du (gasnu's :assert REPL command and the lasna assert-fact WIT method) run logji inside the WASM sandbox, where a stack overflow is a recoverable Wasmtime trap rather than a host process kill — exactly the containment the Part IV sandboxing exists to provide. The native host-process crash is reachable only through nibli-engine's assert_fact_direct embedding API and the engine's own tests, which the project classifies as an internal embedding library, not a user-facing production server endpoint. So: real crash and real defect, but narrower reachability than the reported server-DoS framing.

partial.title: Compute-backend trust puncture: unauthenticated backend results auto-asserted as permanent KB facts, with no cache invalidation
partial.detail: When a ComputeNode evaluation gets 'true' from the external backend, the result is auto-asserted as a PERMANENT ground fact mid-query (logji/src/reasoning.rs:284-299) — over a plaintext, unauthenticated TCP JSON-Lines channel (gasnu/src/main.rs:442-456). A malicious or MITM'd backend injects arbitrary 'facts' that downstream rules chain on: the headline 'every conclusion formally derived' does not hold across this undocumented trust boundary. The ingestion also skips predicate-cache invalidation, violating the documented invariant at kb.rs:871 (a False cached earlier in the same query stays authoritative after ingestion makes the fact derivable). The reference backend widens the surface: handle_tenfa computes unbounded bigint exponentiation (single request burns CPU/RAM superlinearly), one unbounded thread per connection, and malformed JSON escapes the error handler and kills the connection thread (python/nibli_backend.py:78-83, 130-176) — all reachable under the documented --host 0.0.0.0.
sources: python compute backend + Transparency Triad server/UI
partial.check_hint: Point NIBLI_COMPUTE_ADDR at a mock backend returning {"result":true} for an arbitrary predicate; confirm the fact persists in the KB and feeds rule chaining. Send tenfa with exponent 2e8 to the python backend and watch CPU/RAM.

adjudication.evidence:
CORE MECHANISM CONFIRMED. logji/src/reasoning.rs:284-300: on a backend `true` (and the arithmetic branch), the ComputeNode result is auto-asserted mid-query via `assert_typed_fact(fact, inner)`. logji/src/rules.rs:384-476: assert_typed_fact inserts into fact_store and triggers forward rules but NEVER calls invalidate_pred_cache(), whereas every user-facing mutation does (logji/src/lib.rs:105,153,170,219,583). The channel is plaintext, unauthenticated TCP: gasnu/src/main.rs:101-127 just `TcpStream::connect(addr)` (no TLS/auth/integrity), addr from NIBLI_COMPUTE_ADDR (main.rs:655). A malicious/MITM backend returning {"result":true} for an arbitrary predicate thus injects a ground fact that downstream rules chain on — puncturing "every conclusion formally derived" across an undocumented trust boundary. Note the backend is operator-configured (intended TCB member), but plaintext+no-integrity is below the Part IV "production engineering" bar and the boundary is undocumented.

CACHE-INVALIDATION BREACH CONFIRMED AS CODE, observable-harm UNPROVEN. The mid-query compute assert bypasses the invariant documented at kb.rs:871 ("Call after any KB mutation ... to prevent stale cached results"). Iterative deepening clears the cache once (lib.rs:305) then re-enables without clearing each pass (lib.rs:289), so a definitive False cached in an early pass survives into later passes after the compute assert. BUT the compute fact itself is protected by the `typed_fact_is_asserted` check at reasoning.rs:578 which precedes the cache lookup; poisoning can only bite a DOWNSTREAM derived fact, which is order-dependent and I did not demonstrate end-to-end.

PYTHON BACKEND (probed live): (1) float**float overflow — `2.0**2e8` raises OverflowError, NOT in the handler tuple (ValueError,TypeError,ZeroDivisionError) at nibli_backend.py:145, and not caught by the outer `except (ConnectionResetError,BrokenPipeError)` at :151, so a single legitimate tenfa request (engine always sends floats) crashes the connection thread. (2) Non-object JSON (`123`, `[1,2]`, `"hi"`, `null`, `true`) passes json.loads (escapes the JSONDecodeError guard at :124) then hits `request.get(...)` at :130 -> uncaught AttributeError -> thread dies; confirms the reviewer's "escapes error handler / kills thread" effect (their "malformed JSON" wording is imprecise). (3) one daemon thread per connection, no cap (:172-176); `--host 0.0.0.0` documented (:17,:185).

OVERSTATED/REFUTED: the "handle_tenfa unbounded bigint ... burns CPU/RAM superlinearly" headline does not hold — from the engine (floats) it is an instant uncaught OverflowError (thread death, no resource blowup); from a raw adversarial integer-JSON client, int**int is bounded (2**2e7 = 2.5MB in 0.03s, probed) and then math.isclose triggers uncaught OverflowError that kills the thread. So the real defect is uncaught-exception per-connection thread death, not resource exhaustion.
adjudication.fair_wording: Compute-backend trust puncture (PARTIAL — core confirmed, severity framing partly overstated): On a `true` from the operator-configured external backend, logji auto-asserts the result as a ground fact mid-query (reasoning.rs:284-300 -> rules.rs:384/463) over a plaintext, unauthenticated TCP JSON-Lines channel (gasnu/main.rs:101-127, addr from NIBLI_COMPUTE_ADDR). A MITM/malicious backend can return {"result":true} for an arbitrary predicate; downstream rules then chain on it, so "every conclusion formally derived" does not hold across this undocumented, integrity-free trust boundary. The auto-assert path bypasses invalidate_pred_cache (rules.rs:384-476), violating the invariant documented at kb.rs:871 — confirmed as a code/invariant breach, though an observable wrong answer is order-dependent (the compute fact itself is shielded by the asserted-check at reasoning.rs:578; only downstream-derived facts could be poisoned across deepening passes) and was not demonstrated end-to-end. The Python reference backend has real per-connection-thread robustness bugs reachable under the documented --host 0.0.0.0: its handler tuple omits OverflowError (nibli_backend.py:145), so a single large-exponent tenfa request (or any non-object JSON value, which escapes the JSONDecodeError guard and hits an uncaught AttributeError at :130) kills the connection thread; threads are spawned per connection with no cap (:172-176). REFUTED sub-claim: the "unbounded bigint exponentiation burns CPU/RAM superlinearly" framing — the real failure is uncaught-exception thread death, not resource exhaustion (int**int is bounded and sub-second before isclose's uncaught OverflowError kills the thread; float args overflow instantly).

judges.persona: Ana — staff Rust engineer, ten years maintaining large production Rust systems; judges idiom, API design, error handling, maintainability, and whether she would accept stewardship of the codebase.
judges.verdict: Nibli has the bones of a serious system — the WIT flat-buffer contract is specification-grade, the four-valued QueryResult lattice gets a soundness subtlety right (resource exhaustion can never launder into negation-as-failure) that most homegrown provers fumble, and the fail-before-fix regression culture is better than most funded teams manage. But the engine's single non-negotiable promise — "every conclusion is formally derived" — is broken by a pervasive fail-open idiom: `_ => None` catch-alls in rule compilation that silently drop antecedents and register unconditional rules; assertions that ingest zero facts yet return a fact id and list as active; a flattener that snapshots `sentences.len()` before recursing (gerna/src/lib.rs:244) so nested abstractions silently bind the wrong body; a lexer that discards everything after the first unlexable character with no error. In a zero-hallucination engine, every silent drop is a hallucination with a proof trace attached. The irony is structural: the one place the code fails closed — the implicit-ke'a ambiguity firewall — is exactly the pattern the rest of the compiler needed and didn't get. Compounding this, the Part IV production claims fail on both canonical runtimes: the HTTP server runs reasoning natively with no fuel, no memory cap, and uncancellable spawn_blocking threads against a known non-terminating query class, while the REPL's WASM session bricks permanently after its first trap with no re-instantiation path; persistence is broken at every layer (retraction never persisted, replay fact-ID drift, a WasiFactStore unreachable through its own trait); and Chapter 20's "captured verbatim" GDPR outputs cannot be reproduced against the shipped corpus. The dual-evaluator core that runs both traced and untraced inference and discards one verdict (`let _ = holds`) is the architectural tell: for an engine whose thesis is that the proof IS the answer, the proof and the answer are not even checked for agreement. This is a gifted single author's ambitious, well-documented prototype wearing production clothing — I would accept stewardship of the architecture, but not of the contract it currently fails to keep.
judges.would_merge: No — not for production use in its current state. Gates, in order: (1) Make the entire assert/compile path fail closed: any antecedent atom hitting build_rule_template_fact's `_ => None`, and any assertion producing zero facts and zero rules (Xor, bare disjunction, negated ground facts, rel clauses on la/le/pro-sumti), must return a typed nibli-error instead of success — enforced by a property test asserting "assert returns Ok ⟹ the same sentence queries True or a rule was registered". (2) Fix the four front-end silent-corruption bugs with pinning tests: lexer error channel instead of truncation at first unlexable char; use push_sentence's return value in the Abstraction/Restricted flattener arms; stop connective re-pairing across failed sentences in recovery; RAII depth guard. (3) Fix the best_result wipe (reasoning.rs:823/968) and the du-fallback unbounded recursion; unify or cross-assert the traced/untraced evaluators (delete `let _ = holds`). (4) Consolidate pipeline glue: nibli-engine as the single orchestrator, lasna a thin WIT adapter; fix or quarantine the go'i snapshot drain that traps the whole session. (5) Make persistence honest: persist retraction through the engine API, replay via assert_fact_with_id everywhere, fix the gasnu fact-ID drift and redb row duplication, delete or finish WasiFactStore, deny the unused-Result warning. (6) Bound the server path (run queries through the fueled WASM component or add a watchdog; keyed rate limiting; GraphQL depth/complexity limits) and re-instantiate the gasnu session after traps. (7) CI must execute the lasna WASM component with behavioral fuel/memory-exhaustion tests, and integration tests must run the chapter-narrated queries against the shipped corpora (which requires fixing the GDPR full-corpus hang). (8) Mark compute-backend-derived facts with distinct provenance and invalidate the predicate cache on auto-assert. Gates 1-3 are non-negotiable soundness; I'd merge incrementally behind them.
top_strengths: Boundary design: wit/world.wit's flat u32-indexed parallel-array buffers with resource lifecycles, one shared typed error variant, and doc comments readable as a specification — backed by exhaustive match-based conversions so cross-representation drift is a compile error.

The four-valued QueryResult lattice with definitive-only predicate tabling (reasoning.rs:634-643): depth/cycle verdicts are never cached and never collapse into False, so fuel limits cannot be laundered into false negations — a subtle soundness property implemented consistently and pinned by tests documenting the exact poisoning scenario each prevents.

Regression and negative-control discipline unusual for a single author: every shipped soundness fix carries a test that failed pre-fix, and integration tests use discriminating two-sided controls (ddi_toxicity_requires_both_conditions) that detect conjunction degeneration — mutation-testing thinking done by hand.
top_weaknesses: Pervasive fail-open compilation in a soundness engine: unrepresentable rule antecedents silently dropped yielding unconditional rules that derive consequents from an empty KB; assertions that ingest nothing yet return success with a fact id; meaning silently discarded across smuni (rel clauses on names, connected sumti under tags, FA overflow) and gerna (lexer truncation, wrong abstraction body indices, connective re-pairing) — each a probe-confirmed path to asserting or affirming something the user never wrote.

Production-hardening claims fail on both canonical runtimes: nibli-server runs reasoning natively with no fuel/memory bound and uncancellable blocking threads (cosmetic 408 timeout, global unkeyed rate limiter, unbounded GraphQL); gasnu's component instance is permanently bricked after any fuel/memory trap; belief-revision durability is broken at every persistence layer.

Duplication-driven semantic drift at the core: two pipeline-glue implementations diverge between REPL and server surfaces (go'i, parse-error policy), three parallel evaluators agree only by discipline with the traced verdict literally discarded, and seven IR representations make one variant addition an ~8-site, 6-crate change — the rot vector that will dominate maintenance cost.

judges.persona: Dr. Okafor — logic-programming implementer (Prolog/Datalog engines, tabling, negation-as-failure, WAM lineage); judging the reasoning core for soundness and whether the engine's guarantees are real
judges.verdict: Nibli gets right the one thing most homegrown provers get wrong — its four-valued verdict lattice with definitive-only tabling (logji/src/reasoning.rs:634-643) means depth exhaustion and cycle cuts can never be laundered into a negation-as-failure "proof", and its condition-side-pattern-variable vs conclusion-side-SkolemFn split handles antecedent/consequent existentials with textbook precision. But nearly everything around that core fails open: the rule compiler silently drops antecedent atoms it cannot represent and registers the rule anyway (rules.rs:677-686, 1048) — so `ganai ga A gi B gi C` derives C on an empty KB; assertions that ingest zero facts return success with a fact id; the lexer truncates at the first unlexable character without a word; the flattener silently binds abstraction bodies to the wrong sentence; relative clauses on names vanish; and in the evaluator itself a later failing rule wipes a pending ResourceExceeded into a cached definitive False that NAF flips to a wrong True (reasoning.rs:791/823), while the du-equivalence fallback stack-overflows the process and the stratification guard accepts an unstratifiable program one run in three depending on HashMap order. In tabled-resolution engineering the rule is simple: a prover that cannot represent a formula must reject it, never approximate it. Nibli's slogan is "every conclusion is formally derived"; as shipped, that holds on the well-tested happy path and is falsified by probe on a dozen silent paths spanning every pipeline stage — and the production story (persistence that resurrects retracted facts, an unbounded uncancellable server path, a WASM runtime with zero behavioral tests, book case-study queries that do not reproduce against the shipped corpus) does not survive contact either. This is a talented single author building real machinery — the cache discipline and regression culture are better than most graduate-level engines I have reviewed — wrapped in a guarantee the codebase does not yet earn.
judges.would_merge: No — not in its current state. Gates before sign-off: (1) Fail-closed compilation everywhere: reject any assertion where an antecedent or consequent atom fails build_rule_template_fact (rules.rs:1048 `_ => None` must propagate an error, not a skip), and reject any assertion whose processing yields zero facts and zero rules (Xor, bare disjunction, negated ground facts); pin each with the dossier's probes as regression tests. (2) Fix the best_result `and_then` wipe in all four rule-loop variants (reasoning.rs:791/823/937/968) so a later definitively-failing rule never erases a pending ResourceExceeded, plus a competing-rules regression test; add a cycle/depth guard to the du-equivalence fallback (reasoning.rs:594-621). (3) Make the stratification check deterministic and position-accurate (in-cycle negative-edge tracking, root-order independence). (4) Front-end honesty: lexer must error on unlexable input instead of truncating; flattener must use push_sentence's return value for Abstraction/Restricted body indices; recovery must not re-pair connectives across failed sentences; depth counters must roll back on error paths. (5) smuni: attach-or-reject relative clauses on non-quantifier sumti (la/le/pro-sumti), fix tagged-connected-sumti right-operand loss, error on FA-overflow argument drops. (6) Atomic assertion (no orphaned store mutations when a later root fails) and multiplicity-aware retraction; fix or excise the persistence layer (engine retraction durability, gasnu replay ID drift, dead WasiFactStore, unbounded typed-redb growth). (7) Consolidate to one pipeline orchestrator (nibli-engine, with lasna as a thin WIT adapter) and replace `let _ = holds` with a traced/untraced verdict-consistency assertion. (8) CI must build and behaviorally exercise the lasna WASM component (fuel exhaustion, memory cap, post-trap recovery or re-instantiation), and the GDPR/DDI chapter queries must reproduce against the shipped corpora within a time bound. (9) Bound and cancel server-side reasoning (run queries in the sandboxed component or add a native fuel analogue; abortable execution instead of pinned spawn_blocking threads). Items 1-4 are the soundness-critical core; I would not accept the zero-hallucination claim in print until they land with pinning tests.
top_strengths: Four-valued QueryResult lattice with definitive-only tabling, cross-depth cache persistence, and invalidation-on-mutation — resource limits and cycle cuts structurally cannot masquerade as NAF falsity, a subtle property most homegrown provers (and some published ones) get wrong, implemented consistently across both evaluators and iterative deepening.

The typed rule-template machinery: condition-side existentials as pattern variables vs conclusion-side existentials as SkolemFn (with DepPair multi-dependency), unified structurally via unify_facts over typed GroundTerms — the semantically correct asymmetric treatment of Neo-Davidsonian event variables, verified end-to-end by multi-hop probes.

Genuine regression discipline and boundary design: every shipped soundness fix is pinned by a test that failed pre-fix, integration tests use discriminating negative controls (apixaban/CYP3A4, conjunction gates), and the flat u32-indexed WIT contract (ast-buffer/logic-buffer/proof-trace) is specification-grade with compiler-enforced exhaustive conversions.
top_weaknesses: Systemic fail-open behavior contradicting the engine's only product — its soundness guarantee: the rule compiler registers unconditional rules when antecedent atoms are unrepresentable (empty-KB derivations from ganai with disjunctive/tensed/nested antecedents), assertions ingesting zero facts return success with a fact id, and gerna/smuni silently truncate, mis-index, or drop meaning (lexer truncation, flattener body-index corruption, vanishing relative clauses on names) — roughly a dozen probe-confirmed silent-unsoundness paths across every pipeline stage.

Core evaluator defects a Datalog engineer would block release on: the best_result and_then wipe turns an entailed query into a cached definitive False that NAF inverts to a wrong True; the du-equivalence fallback recurses unboundedly to a process-killing stack overflow; the NAF stratification guard is HashMap-iteration-order nondeterministic (~1/3 acceptance of an unstratifiable program).

The production-engineering claims are not delivered where users touch the system: belief-revision durability is broken at every persistence layer (retracted facts resurrect, replay IDs drift, the WASI store is unreachable through its own trait), the canonical HTTP server runs unbounded uncancellable native reasoning behind a cosmetic timeout, one trap permanently bricks the gasnu session, the WASM runtime has zero behavioral tests, and the book's GDPR chapter outputs do not reproduce against the shipped corpus.

judges.persona: Sam — production-infrastructure and security engineer; judges deployability: sandboxing, resource limits, failure modes, persistence integrity, trust boundaries, and what breaks at 3am
judges.verdict: Nibli is the rare book companion where the sandbox is actually wired — gasnu's WASI ctx really is stdout/stderr-only, fuel really is refueled per command, memory really is capped via StoreLimits, and the four-valued result lattice genuinely prevents resource exhaustion from laundering into proofs. But held to its own "production engineering" claim, it fails on both runtimes users actually touch. The hardened sandbox guards a local REPL that permanently bricks ("cannot enter component instance") the first time its own fuel limit fires — no re-instantiation path, and the trap is misclassified as [Host Error] because the substring match runs on a context line that never contains "fuel". Meanwhile the exposed GraphQL server runs the reasoner native with no fuel, no memory cap, a global unkeyed rate limiter, and a 30-second "timeout" that 408s the client while the uncancellable spawn_blocking query — including a known-filed non-terminating one — pins a blocking-pool thread forever. Persistence corrupts across restarts (engine retractions resurrect, replay fact-ID drift silently overwrites live redb records, the typed store grows unboundedly per restart), and the compute backend is a plaintext unauthenticated TCP oracle whose every {"result":true} is auto-asserted as a permanent ground fact in a "zero-hallucination" KB — a trust puncture through the engine's entire thesis. Underneath, the firewall fails open in at least six independently confirmed silent-wrong-answer classes reachable from ordinary surface Lojban (unconditional rules from unrepresentable antecedents, flattener body-index corruption, success-with-fact-id for assertions that ingest nothing, lexer truncation at the first unlexable character, dropped relative clauses on names, the best_result wipe returning definitive False for entailed queries). The engineering culture — fail-before-fix regression pinning, two-sided negative controls, a specification-grade WIT contract, an honest todo.md — is genuinely above average for a single author, and the bones are worth fixing. But "every conclusion is formally derived" is the product, and today the system can silently assert truncated input, fire rules with dropped antecedents, brick its hardened runtime on its own limits, and resurrect retracted facts on restart. The production claim is aspiration, not yet fact.
judges.would_merge: No sign-off for production use today. Gates, in order: (1) Fail-closed ingestion — reject any assertion whose rule-antecedent atoms cannot be represented (rules.rs:1048 `_ => None` silently skipped at 677-686) and any assertion that ingests zero facts and zero rules yet returns a fact id (Xor/bare-Or/negated ground facts); the zero-hallucination contract requires refusing what it cannot represent. (2) Fix the gerna silent-corruption set with index-target regression tests: flattener body indices (one line per arm at gerna/src/lib.rs:244/320), a lexer error channel instead of truncation-at-first-bad-char, connective re-pairing across failed sentences, depth-guard reset on error paths. (3) gasnu trap recovery — re-instantiate the component (or use a per-command store) after any trap, fix the [Limit] misclassification, and add behavioral fuel/memory-exhaustion tests; `just ci` must build and execute the lasna WASM component, which today has zero automated execution. (4) Bound the exposed server path — run query_with_kb under the same fueled/memory-capped WASM host as gasnu or add real cancellation (abortable execution, not a cosmetic 408), keyed rate limiting, GraphQL depth/complexity limits and introspection off, fix the RATE_LIMIT_RPS=0 startup panic. (5) Close the compute trust puncture — authenticate/integrity-protect the channel, and record backend-derived facts as externally-sourced in provenance instead of asserting them as permanent ground truth (also fix the cache-invalidation bypass). (6) Persistence integrity — persist retractions through the engine API, replay by original fact ID end-to-end (expose assert_fact_with_id via WIT for gasnu), fix redb duplicate-row growth and ID-collision overwrite, and make the chapter-narrated GDPR queries actually reproduce against the shipped gdpr.lojban in CI. (7) Reasoning-core fixes with pinning tests: the best_result ResourceExceeded wipe (reasoning.rs:791/823/937/968), the unbounded du-equivalence recursion, the lasna go'i snapshot drain that traps the session, and the retraction/registry consistency holes (duplicate-fact conflation, non-atomic multi-root assertion). Until (1)-(4) land, this should not face untrusted input; until (5)-(6) land, it should not own durable state.
top_strengths: gasnu host hardening is genuinely implemented, not narrated: minimal WASI capability surface (stdout/stderr only — no fs, no net, no env), per-command fuel refuel, live-updatable StoreLimits memory caps, and a mock-TCP-tested lazy-reconnect compute client — the one place the Part IV claims are real, and rare even in professional codebases.

The four-valued QueryResult lattice with definitive-only tabling makes 'resource limits never masquerade as proofs' an architectural property: depth/fuel exhaustion propagates as ResourceExceeded through every connective and negation, and only context-independent True/False are cached — a subtle soundness invariant most homegrown provers get wrong, here handled precisely and regression-pinned.

Verification culture well above single-author norm: every shipped soundness fix is pinned by a test that failed pre-fix, integration tests use discriminating two-sided negative controls (apixaban/CYP3A4, conjunction gates), and the wit/world.wit contract is documented to specification grade with compiler-enforced exhaustive conversions.
top_weaknesses: The zero-hallucination firewall fails open across the stack: at least six confirmed silent-wrong-answer classes reachable from ordinary input — unconditional rules registered from unrepresentable antecedents, flattener body-index miscompilation, assertions that ingest nothing but return success with a fact id, lexer truncation discarding everything after the first unlexable character, relative clauses on names silently dropped, and an entailed query returning cached definitive False. The product invariant is violated silently, which is the one failure mode the book forbids.

The hardening protects the wrong runtime: the exposed HTTP server (nibli-server) runs reasoning native with no fuel/memory bound, an uncancellable cosmetic timeout (a filed non-terminating query pins a blocking thread per request until pool exhaustion), and a global unkeyed rate limiter — while the actually-sandboxed REPL permanently bricks its component instance on its own fuel trap with no recovery path, and the WASM runtime plus fuel/memory enforcement have zero behavioral tests in CI.

State and trust boundaries leak: belief-revision durability is broken at every persistence layer (engine retractions resurrect on restart, replay fact-ID drift silently overwrites live records, unbounded typed-redb growth, a dead-code WASI store contradicting the book), and the compute backend is an unauthenticated plaintext TCP oracle whose 'true' responses are auto-asserted as permanent KB facts mid-query, bypassing cache invalidation.
